248	/// Runs the full compilation pipeline on collected functions.
249	///
250	/// # Pipeline Steps
251	///
252	/// 1. Register the `dialect-mir`, `dialect-nvvm`, and LLVM dialects
253	/// 2. Translate each function's MIR body into `dialect-mir`
254	/// 3. Verify the `dialect-mir` module
255	/// 4. Unless full variable-debug mode is enabled, run `mem2reg` to promote slot
256	///    allocas back into SSA
257	/// 5. In the same modes, unroll annotated loops and clean up changed functions
258	/// 6. Lower `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect (via `mir-lower`)
259	/// 7. Verify the LLVM dialect module
260	/// 8. Export the LLVM dialect to a `.ll` file (including device extern declarations)
261	/// 9. Invoke `llc` to generate PTX (or emit LTOIR/NVVM IR when requested)
262	///
263	/// # Target Selection
264	///
265	/// Automatically detects GPU features (WGMMA, TMA, tcgen05) and selects
266	/// an appropriate SM target. Can be overridden via `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET`.
267	///
268	/// # Device Externs
269	///
270	/// External device function declarations (from `#[device] extern "C" { ... }`)
271	/// are emitted as LLVM `declare` statements. These are resolved at link time
272	/// by nvJitLink when linking with external LTOIR (e.g., CCCL libraries).
273	///
274	/// # Errors
275	///
276	/// Returns [`PipelineError`] with details on which step failed.
277	pub fn run_pipeline(
278	    functions: &[CollectedFunction],
279	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
280	    config: &PipelineConfig,
281	) -> Result<CompilationResult, PipelineError> {
282	    prepare_output_dir(&config.output_dir)?;
283	
284	    let mut ctx = Context::new();
285	
286	    // Step 1: Register dialects
287	    crate::translator::register_dialects(&mut ctx);
288	
289	    // Step 2: Create module
290	    let module_name: pliron::identifier::Identifier = config
291	        .output_name
292	        .clone()
293	        .try_into()
294	        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "kernel".try_into().unwrap());
295	    let module = pliron::builtin::ops::ModuleOp::new(&mut ctx, module_name);
296	    let module_op_ptr = module.get_operation();
297	
298	    let mut legaliser = Legaliser::default();
299	
300	    // Step 3: Translate all functions
301	    for func in functions {
302	        if config.verbose {
303	            eprintln!(
304	                "Translating {}: {}",
305	                if func.is_kernel {
306	                    "kernel"
307	                } else {
308	                    "device fn"
309	                },
310	                func.export_name
311	            );
312	        }
313	
314	        let body = func
315	            .instance
316	            .body()
317	            .ok_or_else(|| PipelineError::NoBody(func.export_name.clone()))?;
318	
319	        let func_op_ptr = crate::translator::body::translate_body(
320	            &mut ctx,
321	            &body,
322	            &func.instance,
323	            func.is_kernel,
324	            func.is_inline_always,
325	            Some(&func.export_name),
326	            &mut legaliser,
327	            config.debug_kind,
328	            func.debug_source_scopes.as_ref(),
329	        )
330	        .map_err(|e| {
331	            // Use .disp(&ctx) for rich error formatting with location and backtrace
332	            PipelineError::Translation(format!("{}: {}", func.export_name, e.disp(&ctx)))
333	        })?;
334	
335	        // Dump the per-function IR BEFORE verification so users can see
336	        // what the translator produced even when verification fails. If we
337	        // verified first and bailed, `--show-mir-dialect` / `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_MIR`
338	        // would silently print nothing for the offending function.
339	        if config.show_mir_dialect {
340	            eprintln!(
341	                "\n=== dialect-mir func: {} (pre-verify) ===",
342	                func.export_name
343	            );
344	            eprintln!("{}", func_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
345	        }
346	
347	        verify_operation(&ctx, func_op_ptr, &func.export_name)?;
348	
349	        // Append to module
350	        append_to_module(&ctx, module_op_ptr, func_op_ptr);
351	    }
352	
353	    // Step 4: Verify module. Dump BEFORE verify so module-level verification
354	    // failures still surface the consolidated IR to the user.
355	    if config.show_mir_dialect {
356	        eprintln!("\n=== dialect-mir module (pre-verify) ===");
357	        eprintln!("{}", module_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
358	    }
359	    if config.verbose {
360	        eprintln!("\n=== Verifying dialect-mir module ===");
361	    }
362	    verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "module")?;
363	    if config.verbose {
364	        eprintln!("dialect-mir verification successful ✓");
365	    }
366	
367	    // Step 4.5: Run mem2reg (promote `mir.alloca` + `mir.load`/`mir.store`
368	    // chains back to SSA values).
369	    //
370	    // Full-debug is a `-G`-style build: we keep every source local in its stack
371	    // slot so cuda-gdb can read it from a stable memory location for the whole
372	    // scope (via `llvm.dbg.declare`). Promoting locals to SSA would narrow each
373	    // variable's inspectable range to its register's liveness, which is why an
374	    // optimized `dbg.value` build shows `<optimized out>` for in-scope locals.
375	    // We therefore skip mem2reg whenever variable info is requested. The
376	    // promotion-aware `mir.dbg_value` salvage (see `dialect-mir::ops::debug`)
377	    // remains the mechanism for any future optimized-debug tier that *does*
378	    // promote.
379	    if config.debug_kind.variables_enabled() {
380	        if config.verbose {
381	            eprintln!("\n=== Skipping mem2reg (full debug keeps locals in memory) ===");
382	        }
383	    } else {
384	        if config.verbose {
385	            eprintln!("\n=== Running mem2reg ===");
386	        }
387	        // pliron's pass infra now threads an AnalysisManager through mem2reg
388	        // (caches dominator trees etc.); we run it standalone, so a fresh empty
389	        // manager suffices. The returned IRStatus (Changed/Unchanged) is discarded.
390	        let mut analyses = pliron::pass_manager::AnalysisManager::default();
391	        pliron::opts::mem2reg::mem2reg(module_op_ptr, &mut ctx, &mut analyses).map_err(|e| {
392	            PipelineError::Verification {
393	                name: "mem2reg".to_string(),
394	                message: e.disp(&ctx).to_string(),
395	                operation: None,
396	            }
397	        })?;
398	        if config.verbose {
399	            eprintln!("mem2reg successful ✓");
400	        }
401	        if config.show_mir_dialect {
402	            eprintln!("\n=== dialect-mir module (after mem2reg) ===");
403	            eprintln!("{}", module_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
404	        }
405	        verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "module post-mem2reg")?;
406	
407	        // Step 4.6: annotation-driven loop unrolling (#[unroll] / #[unroll(N)]).
408	        // Runs on the SSA form mem2reg just produced; a no-op unless a loop
409	        // contains a `mir.unroll_hint` operation. The pass receives mem2reg's
410	        // AnalysisManager for the standard pass shape, but recomputes dominance
411	        // after each CFG rewrite.
412	        if config.verbose {
413	            eprintln!("\n=== Running loop-unroll ===");
414	        }
415	        mir_transforms::unroll::unroll_annotated_loops(module_op_ptr, &mut ctx, &mut analyses)
416	            .map_err(|e| PipelineError::Verification {
417	                name: "loop-unroll".to_string(),
418	                message: e.disp(&ctx).to_string(),
419	                operation: None,
420	            })?;
421	        verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "module post-unroll")?;
422	        // Constant folding (sccp -> simplify_cfg -> dce) runs inside the unroll
423	        // pass, scoped to functions it actually unrolled; see
424	        // `mir_transforms::unroll`. Non-unrolled kernels are left for `opt`/NVVM.
425	    }
426	
427	    // Step 4.9: Add structured device-extern declarations before call
428	    // lowering. The call converter consults these declarations to preserve
429	    // pointer address spaces and insert an explicit addrspacecast when the
430	    // caller and external ABI differ. Adding declarations only after lowering
431	    // is too late: every unknown pointer argument has already fallen back to
432	    // generic addrspace(0) by then.
433	    if !device_externs.is_empty() {
434	        if config.verbose {
435	            eprintln!(
436	                "\n=== Adding {} device extern declarations ===",
437	                device_externs.len()
438	            );
439	        }
440	        add_device_extern_declarations(&mut ctx, module_op_ptr, device_externs)?;
441	    }
442	
443	    // Step 5: Lower dialect-mir → LLVM dialect.
444	    if config.verbose {
445	        eprintln!("\n=== Lowering dialect-mir → LLVM dialect ===");
446	    }
447	    lower_to_llvm(&mut ctx, module_op_ptr, config.allow_fma_contraction)?;
448	
449	    // Detect CUDA libdevice usage.
450	    //
451	    // Lowering the rustc float-math intrinsics emits `__nv_*` libdevice
452	    // calls (e.g. `__nv_sinf`, `__nv_pow`). `llc` cannot resolve those — they
453	    // need libNVVM + nvJitLink + `libdevice.10.bc`, which the example owns
454	    // (see `examples/device_ffi_test/tools/`). When we see them we:
455	    //   1. Force NVVM IR mode so the `.ll` is suitable for libNVVM input.
456	    //   2. Skip the `llc → .ptx` step, because the resulting PTX would have
457	    //      unresolved `__nv_*` extern calls and `cuModuleLoad` would reject
458	    //      it.
459	    // The example is then expected to feed the `.ll` through the LTOIR
460	    // pipeline (compile_ltoir + link_ltoir) and load the resulting cubin.
461	    let needs_libdevice = module_uses_libdevice(&ctx, module_op_ptr);
462	    let emit_nvvm_ir = config.emit_nvvm_ir || needs_libdevice;
463	    if needs_libdevice && !config.emit_nvvm_ir && config.verbose {
464	        eprintln!(
465	            "\n=== Detected CUDA libdevice (`__nv_*`) calls; \
466	             auto-emitting NVVM IR (skip llc) ==="
467	        );
468	    }
469	
470	    // NVVM IR export must validate its explicit target against the same
471	    // module feature floor as ordinary PTX generation. An ordinary zero-flag
472	    // build can also discover only now that libdevice makes NVVM IR necessary.
473	    // Render one in-memory preview before choosing the final pointer dialect;
474	    // the preview is only inspected and is not written as an artifact.
475	    let automatic_features = if emit_nvvm_ir {
476	        let preview = render_llvm_ir(
477	            &ctx,
478	            module_op_ptr,
479	            device_externs,
480	            false,
481	            None,
482	            config.debug_kind,
483	        )?;
484	        Some(detect_features_in_llvm_text(&preview))
485	    } else {
486	        None
487	    };
488	
489	    // Pre-Blackwell and Blackwell GPUs use different NVVM IR pointer syntax.
490	    // Resolve one concrete target before export and record it with the
491	    // artifact.
492	    let (nvvm_target, nvvm_dialect) = if emit_nvvm_ir {
493	        let target = resolve_nvvm_target(
494	            config.target_arch.as_deref(),
495	            config.device_arch_hint.as_deref(),
496	            automatic_features,
497	        )?;
498	        let dialect = if target.uses_legacy_llvm() {
499	            NvvmIrDialect::LegacyLlvm7
500	        } else {
501	            NvvmIrDialect::Modern
502	        };
503	        validate_nvvm_debug_support(&target, dialect, config.debug_kind)?;
504	        (Some(target), Some(dialect))
505	    } else {
506	        (None, None)
507	    };
508	
509	    // Step 5.5: Convert LLVM operations to the forms supported by the selected
510	    // NVVM dialect, then verify the changed module before text export.
511	    if let Some(dialect) = nvvm_dialect {
512	        if config.verbose {
513	            if dialect == NvvmIrDialect::LegacyLlvm7 {
514	                eprintln!("\n=== Legalizing LLVM dialect for legacy NVVM ===");
515	            } else {
516	                eprintln!("\n=== Legalizing NVVM bit-intrinsic widths ===");
517	            }
518	        }
519	        nvvm_transforms::legalize_for_nvvm(&mut ctx, module_op_ptr, dialect)
520	            .map_err(|error| PipelineError::Lowering(error.disp(&ctx).to_string()))?;
521	    }
522	
523	    // Step 6: Verify the final LLVM dialect module. Dump BEFORE verify so
524	    // verification failures still surface the exact post-legalization IR.
525	    if config.show_llvm_dialect {
526	        eprintln!("\n=== LLVM dialect (pre-verify) ===");
527	        eprintln!("{}", module_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
528	    }
529	    if config.verbose {
530	        eprintln!("=== Verifying LLVM dialect module ===");
531	    }
532	    verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "llvm module")?;
533	    if config.verbose {
534	        eprintln!("LLVM dialect verification successful ✓");
535	    }
536	
537	    // Step 7: Export to LLVM IR
538	    if config.verbose {
539	        let mode = if emit_nvvm_ir { "NVVM IR" } else { "PTX" };
540	        eprintln!("\n=== Exporting to LLVM IR ({} mode) ===", mode);
541	    }
542	    let ll_path = config.output_dir.join(format!("{}.ll", config.output_name));
543	    // Remove artifacts from earlier builds so changing output mode cannot
544	    // leave older PTX, LTOIR, or cubin selected by the loader.
545	    clear_stale_compilation_artifacts(&config.output_dir, &config.output_name)?;
546	    let _llvm_ir = export_llvm_ir(
547	        &ctx,
548	        module_op_ptr,
549	        device_externs,
550	        &ll_path,
551	        emit_nvvm_ir,
552	        nvvm_dialect,
553	        config.debug_kind,
554	    )?;
555	    if config.verbose {
556	        eprintln!("LLVM IR written to {}", ll_path.display());
557	    }
558	
559	    // Step 8: Generate PTX or stop at NVVM IR for libNVVM-owned paths.
560	    if emit_nvvm_ir {
561	        // Skip llc. Return a would-be ptx_path so callers see a stable shape;
562	        // the file does not exist and the consumer must build its own cubin
563	        // from `ll_path` via libNVVM + nvJitLink.
564	        let ptx_path = config
565	            .output_dir
566	            .join(format!("{}.ptx", config.output_name));
567	        if config.verbose {
568	            let reason = if needs_libdevice {
569	                "libdevice present"
570	            } else {
571	                "NVVM IR requested"
572	            };
573	            eprintln!("\n=== Skipping llc ({reason}); consumer owns libNVVM/nvJitLink build ===");
574	        }
575	        let target = nvvm_target
576	            .as_ref()
577	            .expect("NVVM target was resolved before export")
578	            .sm();
579	        write_nvvm_compile_options_sidecar(
580	            &config.output_dir,
581	            &config.output_name,
582	            config.allow_fma_contraction,
583	        )?;
584	        // Publish the target last: its version marker is the completion record
585	        // that says the sibling options file is required.
586	        write_nvvm_target_sidecar(&config.output_dir, &config.output_name, &target)?;
587	        Ok(CompilationResult {
588	            artifact_path: ll_path.clone(),
589	            artifact_kind: CompilationArtifactKind::NvvmIr,
590	            ll_path,
591	            ptx_path,
592	            target,
593	            allow_fma_contraction: config.allow_fma_contraction,
594	        })
595	    } else {
596	        if config.verbose {
597	            eprintln!("\n=== Generating PTX ===");
598	        }
599	        let ptx_path = config
600	            .output_dir
601	            .join(format!("{}.ptx", config.output_name));
602	        let target = generate_ptx(
603	            &ll_path,
604	            &ptx_path,
605	            config.debug_kind,
606	            config.allow_fma_contraction,
607	        )?;
608	        if config.verbose {
609	            eprintln!(
610	                "✓ PTX written to {} (target: {})",
611	                ptx_path.display(),
612	                target
613	            );
614	        }
615	
616	        Ok(CompilationResult {
617	            artifact_path: ptx_path.clone(),
618	            artifact_kind: CompilationArtifactKind::Ptx,
619	            ll_path,
620	            ptx_path,
621	            target,
622	            allow_fma_contraction: config.allow_fma_contraction,
623	        })
624	    }
625	}
626	
627	/// Ensures the configured output directory exists before any emission step.
628	///
629	/// The pipeline writes every generated artifact under `PipelineConfig::output_dir`.
630	/// Creating the directory at the pipeline boundary lets callers provide fresh
631	/// sidecar paths without separately seeding them first.
632	fn prepare_output_dir(output_dir: &Path) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
633	    std::fs::create_dir_all(output_dir).map_err(|e| {
634	        PipelineError::Export(format!(
635	            "failed to create output directory {}: {}",
636	            output_dir.display(),
637	            e
638	        ))
639	    })
640	}
641	
642	/// Returns true when lowering emitted CUDA libdevice calls.
643	///
644	/// Float math intrinsics (sin, cos, exp, log, pow, …) lower to `__nv_*`
645	/// entry points from `libdevice.10.bc`. `llc` cannot resolve these; they
646	/// need libNVVM + nvJitLink + libdevice. When we see any `__nv_*` symbol
647	/// the example owns the LTOIR build (see `examples/device_ffi_test/tools/`).
648	fn module_uses_libdevice(ctx: &Context, module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
649	    op_uses_libdevice(ctx, module_op_ptr)
650	}
651	
652	/// Recursively scan for declared or called CUDA libdevice functions.
653	fn op_uses_libdevice(ctx: &Context, op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
654	    if let Some(func) = Operation::get_op::<llvm_export::ops::FuncOp>(op_ptr, ctx)
655	        && func.get_symbol_name(ctx).starts_with("__nv_")
656	    {
657	        return true;
658	    }
659	
660	    if let Some(call) = Operation::get_op::<llvm_export::ops::CallOp>(op_ptr, ctx)
661	        && let CallOpCallable::Direct(callee) = call.callee(ctx)
662	        && callee.to_string().starts_with("__nv_")
663	    {
664	        return true;
665	    }
666	
667	    let op_ref = op_ptr.deref(ctx);
668	    for region in op_ref.regions() {
669	        let region_ref = region.deref(ctx);
670	        for block in region_ref.iter(ctx) {
671	            let block_ref = block.deref(ctx);
672	            for child_op in block_ref.iter(ctx) {
673	                if op_uses_libdevice(ctx, child_op) {
674	                    return true;
675	                }
676	            }
677	        }
678	    }
679	
680	    false
681	}
682	
683	/// Recursively verifies an operation and all nested operations.
684	///
685	/// On failure, attempts to find the innermost failing operation for better
686	/// error messages.
687	fn verify_operation(
688	    ctx: &Context,
689	    op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>,
690	    name: &str,
691	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
692	    if let Err(e) = op_ptr.deref(ctx).verify(ctx) {
693	        // Try to find specific failing operation
694	        if let Some((err_op, err_msg)) = find_inner_verification_error(ctx, op_ptr) {
695	            return Err(PipelineError::Verification {
696	                name: name.to_string(),
697	                message: err_msg,
698	                operation: Some(err_op.deref(ctx).disp(ctx).to_string()),
699	            });
700	        }
701	
702	        // Use .disp(ctx) to get full error with location and backtrace
703	        return Err(PipelineError::Verification {
704	            name: name.to_string(),
705	            message: e.disp(ctx).to_string(),
706	            operation: None,
707	        });
708	    }
709	    Ok(())
710	}
711	
712	/// Inserts a function operation into the module's block.
713	fn append_to_module(ctx: &Context, module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>, func_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>) {
714	    let region = module_op_ptr.deref(ctx).get_region(0).deref(ctx);
715	    let block = region.iter(ctx).next().expect("Module should have a block");
716	    func_op_ptr.insert_at_back(block, ctx);
717	}
718	
719	/// Lowers `dialect-mir` operations to the LLVM dialect.
720	///
721	/// Runs `mir-lower`'s `DialectConversion`-based pass, which converts each
722	/// `dialect-mir`/`dialect-nvvm` op to its LLVM dialect equivalent. The LLVM
723	/// dialect auto-registers when the `Context` is created, so no explicit
724	/// registration is needed here.
725	fn lower_to_llvm(
726	    ctx: &mut Context,
727	    module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>,
728	    allow_fma_contraction: bool,
729	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
730	    mir_lower::register(ctx);
731	
732	    match mir_lower::lower_mir_to_llvm_with_options(
733	        ctx,
734	        module_op_ptr,
735	        mir_lower::LoweringOptions {
736	            allow_fma_contraction,
737	        },
738	    ) {
739	        Ok(()) => Ok(()),
740	        // Format with `ctx` so the failing op's location/span survives.
741	        Err(e) => Err(PipelineError::Lowering(e.disp(ctx).to_string())),
742	    }
743	}
744	
745	/// Adds device extern function declarations to the LLVM dialect module.
746	///
747	/// Creates LLVM dialect `FuncOp` declarations (without bodies) for each
748	/// device extern function. These declarations ensure that calls to extern
749	/// functions pass verification; the matching `declare` statements with
750	/// attributes are emitted during LLVM IR export.
751	///
752	/// This runs before MIR-to-LLVM call lowering so the call converter can read
753	/// exact parameter address spaces. It is still idempotent with respect to any
754	/// LLVM declaration already present in the mixed module; inserting a second
755	/// `FuncOp` for the same symbol would fail module verification.
756	fn add_device_extern_declarations(
757	    ctx: &mut Context,
758	    module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>,
759	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
760	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
761	    use llvm_export::ops::FuncOp;
762	    use llvm_export::types::FuncType;
763	    use pliron::builtin::type_interfaces::FunctionTypeInterface;
764	    use pliron::identifier::Identifier;
765	    use std::collections::HashMap;
766	
767	    // Get the module's block pointer first (this is a Ptr, not a Ref, so no borrow issues)
768	    let block = {
769	        let region = module_op_ptr.deref(ctx).get_region(0).deref(ctx);
770	        region.iter(ctx).next().expect("Module should have a block")
771	    };
772	
773	    let declared_symbols: HashMap<_, _> = block
774	        .deref(ctx)
775	        .iter(ctx)
776	        .filter_map(|op| {
777	            Operation::get_op::<FuncOp>(op, ctx)
778	                .map(|f| (f.get_symbol_name(ctx).to_string(), f.get_type(ctx)))
779	        })
780	        .collect();
781	
782	    for decl in device_externs {
783	        let param_types: Vec<_> = decl
784	            .param_types
785	            .iter()
786	            .map(|ty| device_extern_type_to_pliron(ctx, ty, false))
787	            .collect::<Result<_, _>>()?;
788	        let return_type = device_extern_type_to_pliron(ctx, &decl.return_type, true)?;
789	
790	        // Create function type (result, args, is_variadic)
791	        let func_type = FuncType::get(ctx, return_type, param_types, false);
792	
793	        if let Some(existing_type) = declared_symbols.get(&decl.export_name) {
794	            let existing_ref = existing_type.deref(ctx);
795	            let existing = &*existing_ref;
796	            let expected_ref = func_type.deref(ctx);
797	            let expected = &*expected_ref;
798	            if existing.result_type() != expected.result_type()
799	                || existing.arg_types() != expected.arg_types()
800	                || existing.is_var_arg() != expected.is_var_arg()
801	            {
802	                return Err(PipelineError::Export(format!(
803	                    "device extern `@{}` conflicts with the call-site declaration: expected `{}`, found `{}`",
804	                    decl.export_name,
805	                    expected_ref.disp(ctx),
806	                    existing_ref.disp(ctx),
807	                )));
808	            }
809	            continue;
810	        }
811	
812	        // Use the original export name (NOT the prefixed name).
813	        // The MIR sees calls to `cuda_oxide_device_extern_<hash>_foo`, but
814	        // mir-lower/convert/ops/call.rs strips the reserved prefix via
815	        // `reserved_oxide_symbols::device_extern_base_name`, so the LLVM IR
816	        // emits `call @foo(...)`. For that to resolve, we declare `@foo` here.
817	        let func_ident: Identifier = decl.export_name.clone().try_into().map_err(|_| {
818	            PipelineError::Export(format!(
819	                "device-extern symbol `{}` cannot be represented by the LLVM dialect",
820	                decl.export_name
821	            ))
822	        })?;
823	
824	        // Create function declaration (no body = declaration)
825	        let func_op = FuncOp::new(ctx, func_ident, func_type);
826	
827	        // Insert at the front of the module (declarations come before definitions)
828	        func_op.get_operation().insert_at_front(block, ctx);
829	    }
830	
831	    Ok(())
832	}
833	
834	/// Convert the structured device-extern ABI type to the opaque-pointer pliron
835	/// LLVM type used for verification and call lowering.
836	fn device_extern_type_to_pliron(
837	    ctx: &mut Context,
838	    ty: &DeviceExternType,
839	    allow_void: bool,
840	) -> Result<pliron::r#type::TypeHandle, PipelineError> {
841	    use llvm_export::types::{ArrayType, HalfType, PointerType, VoidType};
842	    use pliron::builtin::types::{FP32Type, FP64Type, IntegerType, Signedness};
843	
844	    Ok(match ty {
845	        DeviceExternType::Void if allow_void => VoidType::get(ctx).into(),
846	        DeviceExternType::Void => {
847	            return Err(PipelineError::Export(
848	                "device-extern parameters and aggregate elements cannot be `void`".to_string(),
849	            ));
850	        }
851	        DeviceExternType::Integer(bits) if *bits > 0 => {
852	            IntegerType::get(ctx, *bits, Signedness::Signless).into()
853	        }
854	        DeviceExternType::Integer(_) => {
855	            return Err(PipelineError::Export(
856	                "device-extern integer width must be non-zero".to_string(),
857	            ));
858	        }
859	        DeviceExternType::Float16 => HalfType::get(ctx).into(),
860	        DeviceExternType::Float32 => FP32Type::get(ctx).into(),
861	        DeviceExternType::Float64 => FP64Type::get(ctx).into(),
862	        DeviceExternType::Pointer {
863	            pointee,
864	            address_space,
865	        } => {
866	            if matches!(pointee.as_ref(), DeviceExternType::Void) {
867	                return Err(PipelineError::Export(
868	                    "device-extern pointer cannot have `void` as its pointee; use i8".to_string(),
869	                ));
870	            }
871	            PointerType::get(ctx, *address_space).into()
872	        }
873	        DeviceExternType::Array { element, len } => {
874	            let element = device_extern_type_to_pliron(ctx, element, false)?;
875	            ArrayType::get(ctx, element, *len).into()
876	        }
877	    })
878	}
879	
880	fn resolve_nvvm_target(
881	    explicit_target: Option<&str>,
882	    device_arch_hint: Option<&str>,
883	    automatic_features: Option<DetectedFeatures>,
884	) -> Result<CudaArch, PipelineError> {
885	    let parse = |target: &str, source: &str| {
886	        target.parse::<CudaArch>().map_err(|error| {
887	            PipelineError::Export(format!(
888	                "cannot select an NVVM IR dialect from the {source} `{target}`: {error}"
889	            ))
890	        })
891	    };
892	
893	    if let Some(target) = explicit_target {
894	        let parsed = parse(target, "explicit CUDA target")?;
895	        if let Some(features) = automatic_features {
896	            validate_target_features(&parsed, features).map_err(PipelineError::Export)?;
897	        }
898	        return Ok(parsed);
899	    }
900	
901	    if let Some(features) = automatic_features {
902	        if let Some(target) = device_arch_hint {
903	            let parsed = parse(target, "detected GPU architecture")?;
904	            if arch_satisfies(&parsed.sm(), features) {
905	                return Ok(parsed);
906	            }
907	        }
908	        let target = select_target(features).map_err(PipelineError::Export)?;
909	        return parse(target, "feature-based compiler default");
910	    }
911	
912	    if let Some(target) = device_arch_hint {
913	        return parse(target, "detected GPU architecture");
914	    }
915	
916	    Err(PipelineError::Export(
917	        "NVVM IR requires a concrete CUDA target because pre-Blackwell and Blackwell+ \
918	         use different LLVM dialects; pass `cargo oxide ... --arch sm_XX` (or set \
919	         CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET)"
920	            .to_string(),
921	    ))
922	}
923	
924	fn validate_nvvm_debug_support(
925	    target: &CudaArch,
926	    dialect: NvvmIrDialect,
927	    debug_kind: DebugKind,
928	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
929	    if dialect == NvvmIrDialect::LegacyLlvm7 && debug_kind != DebugKind::Off {
930	        return Err(PipelineError::Export(format!(
931	            "legacy LLVM 7 NVVM IR for {} does not yet support cuda-oxide debug metadata; \
932	             rebuild without device debug information",
933	            target.sm()
934	        )));
935	    }
936	    Ok(())
937	}
938	
939	fn write_nvvm_target_sidecar(
940	    output_dir: &Path,
941	    output_name: &str,
942	    target: &str,
943	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
944	    let path = output_dir.join(format!("{output_name}.target"));
945	    std::fs::write(
946	        &path,
947	        format!(
948	            "{target}\n{}\n",
949	            oxide_artifacts::COMPILE_OPTIONS_TARGET_MARKER
950	        ),
951	    )
952	    .map_err(|error| {
953	        PipelineError::Export(format!(
954	            "failed to record NVVM target in {}: {error}",
955	            path.display()
956	        ))
957	    })
958	}
959	
960	fn write_nvvm_compile_options_sidecar(
961	    output_dir: &Path,
962	    output_name: &str,
963	    allow_fma_contraction: bool,
964	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
965	    let path = output_dir.join(format!("{output_name}.options"));
966	    let options =
967	        oxide_artifacts::ArtifactCompileOptions::new().with_fma_contraction(allow_fma_contraction);
968	    std::fs::write(&path, options.sidecar_text()).map_err(|error| {
969	        PipelineError::Export(format!(
970	            "failed to record NVVM compile options in {}: {error}",
971	            path.display()
972	        ))
973	    })
974	}
975	
976	fn clear_stale_compilation_artifacts(
977	    output_dir: &Path,
978	    output_name: &str,
979	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
980	    for suffix in [
981	        "ll",
982	        "ptx",
983	        "target",
984	        "options",
985	        "ltoir",
986	        "cubin",
987	        "cubin.target",
988	    ] {
989	        let path = output_dir.join(format!("{output_name}.{suffix}"));
990	        match std::fs::remove_file(&path) {
991	            Ok(()) => {}
992	            Err(error) if error.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {}
993	            Err(error) => {
994	                return Err(PipelineError::Export(format!(
995	                    "failed to invalidate stale CUDA artifact {}: {error}",
996	                    path.display()
997	                )));
998	            }
999	        }
1000	    }
1001	    Ok(())
1002	}
1003	
1004	/// Exports an LLVM dialect module to textual LLVM IR (`.ll` file).
1005	///
1006	/// Backend configuration is selected based on flags:
1007	/// - `emit_nvvm_ir`: Uses `NvvmExportConfig` for NVVM IR output
1008	/// - Otherwise: Uses default `PtxExportConfig` for standard PTX generation
1009	///
1010	/// Device extern declarations are emitted before the main module content.
1011	fn export_llvm_ir(
1012	    ctx: &Context,
1013	    module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>,
1014	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
1015	    path: &Path,
1016	    emit_nvvm_ir: bool,
1017	    nvvm_dialect: Option<NvvmIrDialect>,
1018	    debug_kind: DebugKind,
1019	) -> Result<String, PipelineError> {
1020	    let llvm_ir = render_llvm_ir(
1021	        ctx,
1022	        module_op_ptr,
1023	        device_externs,
1024	        emit_nvvm_ir,
1025	        nvvm_dialect,
1026	        debug_kind,
1027	    )?;
1028	
1029	    std::fs::write(path, &llvm_ir).map_err(|e| PipelineError::Export(e.to_string()))?;
1030	
1031	    Ok(llvm_ir)
1032	}
1033	
1034	/// Render LLVM text without publishing an artifact.
1035	///
1036	/// Automatic libdevice mode uses this once before NVVM legalization to detect
1037	/// the same target features as the normal PTX path. The final export still
1038	/// happens exactly once, after the target-specific legalization pass.
1039	fn render_llvm_ir(
1040	    ctx: &Context,
1041	    module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>,
1042	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
1043	    emit_nvvm_ir: bool,
1044	    nvvm_dialect: Option<NvvmIrDialect>,
1045	    debug_kind: DebugKind,
1046	) -> Result<String, PipelineError> {
1047	    let module_op = Operation::get_op::<pliron::builtin::ops::ModuleOp>(module_op_ptr, ctx)
1048	        .ok_or_else(|| PipelineError::Export("Not a module op".to_string()))?;
1049	
1050	    let llvm_ir = if emit_nvvm_ir {
1051	        let dialect = nvvm_dialect.ok_or_else(|| {
1052	            PipelineError::Export("NVVM export reached without a selected IR dialect".to_string())
1053	        })?;
1054	        let config = PipelineExportConfig {
1055	            inner: llvm_export::export::NvvmExportConfig::new(dialect),
1056	            debug_kind,
1057	        };
1058	        llvm_export::export::export_module_with_externs(ctx, &module_op, device_externs, &config)
1059	            .map_err(PipelineError::Export)?
1060	    } else {
1061	        let config = PipelineExportConfig {
1062	            inner: llvm_export::export::PtxExportConfig,
1063	            debug_kind,
1064	        };
1065	        llvm_export::export::export_module_with_externs(ctx, &module_op, device_externs, &config)
1066	            .map_err(PipelineError::Export)?
1067	    };
1068	
1069	    Ok(llvm_ir)
1070	}
1071	
1072	struct PipelineExportConfig<C> {
1073	    inner: C,
1074	    debug_kind: DebugKind,
1075	}
1076	
1077	impl<C: ExportBackendConfig> ExportBackendConfig for PipelineExportConfig<C> {
1078	    fn datalayout(&self) -> &str {
1079	        self.inner.datalayout()
1080	    }
1081	
1082	    fn emit_llvm_used(&self) -> bool {
1083	        self.inner.emit_llvm_used()
1084	    }
1085	
1086	    fn emit_nvvmir_version(&self) -> bool {
1087	        self.inner.emit_nvvmir_version()
1088	    }
1089	
1090	    fn nvvmir_version(&self) -> [i32; 4] {
1091	        self.inner.nvvmir_version()
1092	    }
1093	
1094	    fn emit_all_kernel_annotations(&self) -> bool {
1095	        self.inner.emit_all_kernel_annotations()
1096	    }
1097	
1098	    fn emit_ptx_kernel_keyword(&self) -> bool {
1099	        self.inner.emit_ptx_kernel_keyword()
1100	    }
1101	
1102	    fn nvvm_ir_dialect(&self) -> Option<NvvmIrDialect> {
1103	        self.inner.nvvm_ir_dialect()
1104	    }
1105	
1106	    fn debug_kind(&self) -> DebugKind {
1107	        self.debug_kind
1108	    }
1109	}
