1111	/// Checks for WGMMA instructions (Hopper sm_90a only, NOT forward-compatible).
1112	///
1113	/// WGMMA (Warpgroup Matrix Multiply-Accumulate) requires sm_90a specifically.
1114	/// These are NOT forward-compatible - only work on H100/H200.
1115	fn contains_wgmma_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1116	    contents.contains("wgmma.fence")
1117	        || contents.contains("wgmma.commit_group")
1118	        || contents.contains("wgmma.wait_group")
1119	        || contents.contains("wgmma.mma_async")
1120	}
1121	
1122	/// Checks for Thread Block Cluster instructions (sm_90+).
1123	///
1124	/// Cluster features require Hopper (sm_90) or newer:
1125	/// - Cluster special registers (%cluster_ctaid, %cluster_nctaid)
1126	/// - Cluster synchronization (cluster.sync)
1127	/// - Distributed shared memory (mapa.shared::cluster)
1128	fn contains_cluster_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1129	    // Cluster special registers
1130	    contents.contains("cluster_ctaid")
1131	        || contents.contains("cluster_nctaid")
1132	        || contents.contains("cluster_ctarank")
1133	        || contents.contains("cluster_nctarank")
1134	        || contents.contains("%clusterid")
1135	        || contents.contains("%nclusterid")
1136	        || contents.contains("%is_explicit_cluster")
1137	        || contents.contains("!\"cluster_dim_x\"")
1138	        || contents.contains("!\"cluster_dim_y\"")
1139	        || contents.contains("!\"cluster_dim_z\"")
1140	        // Cluster synchronization
1141	        || contents.contains("cluster.sync")
1142	        || contents.contains("barrier.cluster.")
1143	        // Distributed shared memory
1144	        || contents.contains("mapa.shared::cluster")
1145	        || contents.contains(".shared::cluster")
1146	        || contains_cluster_fence_features(contents)
1147	        || contains_cluster_scoped_memory_features(contents)
1148	}
1149	
1150	fn contains_cluster_fence_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1151	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1152	        statement.contains("fence.sc.cluster")
1153	            || statement.contains("fence.acq_rel.cluster")
1154	            || statement.contains("fence.acquire.cluster")
1155	            || statement.contains("fence.release.cluster")
1156	    })
1157	}
1158	
1159	fn contains_cluster_scoped_memory_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1160	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1161	        !statement.contains("multimem.")
1162	            && statement.contains(".cluster.")
1163	            && ["ld.", "st.", "atom.", "red."]
1164	                .iter()
1165	                .any(|mnemonic| statement.contains(mnemonic))
1166	    })
1167	}
1168	
1169	/// Checks the one-way fence semantics added in PTX 8.6.
1170	///
1171	/// Unlike the older `.sc` / `.acq_rel` forms, `.acquire` and `.release`
1172	/// require sm_90 for every scope, not just `.cluster`.
1173	fn contains_fence_acquire_release_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1174	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1175	        statement.contains("fence.acquire.") || statement.contains("fence.release.")
1176	    })
1177	}
1178	
1179	/// Checks the multimem instruction family introduced for sm_90.
1180	///
1181	/// Base forms need PTX 8.1. The pipeline currently has no 8.1 feature switch,
1182	/// so PTX 8.6 is the nearest conservative version supported by LLVM.
1183	fn contains_multimem_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1184	    contents.split(';').any(is_multimem_instruction)
1185	}
1186	
1187	fn is_multimem_instruction(statement: &str) -> bool {
1188	    ["multimem.ld_reduce", "multimem.st", "multimem.red"]
1189	        .iter()
1190	        .any(|instruction| statement.contains(instruction))
1191	}
1192	
1193	/// Checks PTX 8.6 multimem formats that require a Blackwell family target.
1194	fn contains_multimem_blackwell_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1195	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1196	        is_multimem_instruction(statement)
1197	            && [".e4m3", ".e5m2", ".acc::f16"]
1198	                .iter()
1199	                .any(|qualifier| statement.contains(qualifier))
1200	    })
1201	}
1202	
1203	/// Checks the PTX 8.6 floating-point extension to `redux.sync`.
1204	fn contains_redux_f32_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1205	    contents
1206	        .split(';')
1207	        .any(|statement| statement.contains("redux.sync") && statement.contains(".f32"))
1208	}
1209	
1210	/// Checks for forward-compatible instructions whose minimum target is sm_90.
1211	///
1212	/// Keep this category architecture-neutral: unlike WGMMA, these instructions
1213	/// are not Hopper-specific and remain available on newer architectures.
1214	fn contains_sm90_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1215	    ["add.rn.bf16x2", "sub.rn.bf16x2", "mul.rn.bf16x2"]
1216	        .iter()
1217	        .any(|mnemonic| contains_instruction_mnemonic(contents, mnemonic))
1218	        || contains_packed_bf16_atomic_features(contents)
1219	        || contains_stmatrix_features(contents)
1220	        || contains_elect_features(contents)
1221	        || contains_fence_acquire_release_features(contents)
1222	        || contains_multimem_features(contents)
1223	}
1224	
1225	/// Native packed bf16 atomic add was added in PTX 7.8 for sm_90.
1226	fn contains_packed_bf16_atomic_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1227	    contains_instruction_mnemonic(contents, "atom.global.add.noftz.bf16x2")
1228	}
1229	
1230	/// Packed f16 atomic add needs PTX 6.2. Its hardware floor predates
1231	/// cuda-oxide's Volta floor, so only the independent PTX ISA requirement must
1232	/// be raised.
1233	fn contains_packed_f16_atomic_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1234	    contains_instruction_mnemonic(contents, "atom.global.add.noftz.f16x2")
1235	}
1236	
1237	fn contains_elect_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1238	    contents.contains("elect.sync")
1239	}
1240	
1241	/// Checks for the register-only 8x8 matrix transpose (PTX 7.8, sm_75+).
1242	fn contains_movmatrix_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1243	    contains_instruction_mnemonic(contents, "movmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n8.trans.b16")
1244	}
1245	
1246	/// Checks the dense BF16 MMA form added by the typed device intrinsic.
1247	///
1248	/// MMA shapes and types have different architecture and PTX ISA floors, so
1249	/// this intentionally matches the complete operation-specific mnemonic.
1250	fn contains_mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1251	    contains_instruction_mnemonic(
1252	        contents,
1253	        "mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.bf16.bf16.f32",
1254	    )
1255	}
1256	
1257	/// Checks for the Ampere FP64 tensor-core MMA operation (PTX 7.0, sm_80+).
1258	fn contains_mma_m8n8k4_f64_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1259	    contains_instruction_mnemonic(contents, "mma.sync.aligned.m8n8k4.row.col.f64.f64.f64.f64")
1260	}
1261	
1262	fn contains_instruction_mnemonic(contents: &str, mnemonic: &str) -> bool {
1263	    contents.match_indices(mnemonic).any(|(index, _)| {
1264	        let preceding = &contents[..index];
1265	        let following = &contents[index + mnemonic.len()..];
1266	        let escapes = ["\\09", "\\0A", "\\0B", "\\0C", "\\0D"];
1267	        // Use PTX token delimiters rather than treating arbitrary punctuation
1268	        // as a boundary. In particular, `$` and `%` participate in PTX
1269	        // identifiers, and guarded opcodes have whitespace after `@{!}p`.
1270	        let begins_at_instruction_boundary = preceding.is_empty()
1271	            || preceding
1272	                .chars()
1273	                .next_back()
1274	                .is_some_and(|ch| ch.is_whitespace() || matches!(ch, '"' | ';' | ':' | '{' | '}'))
1275	            || escapes.iter().any(|escape| preceding.ends_with(escape))
1276	            || preceding.ends_with("*/");
1277	        let ends_at_instruction_boundary =
1278	            following.chars().next().is_some_and(char::is_whitespace)
1279	                || escapes.iter().any(|escape| following.starts_with(escape));
1280	        begins_at_instruction_boundary && ends_at_instruction_boundary
1281	    })
1282	}
1283	
1284	/// Checks the full PTX instruction families, including inline `ptx_asm!`
1285	/// forms that cuda-oxide does not yet expose as typed wrappers.
1286	///
1287	/// Broad family matching is intentional. Missing a valid spelling can
1288	/// silently select an architecture or PTX ISA that is too old; an invalid
1289	/// spelling still reaches ptxas and fails there after conservative targeting.
1290	fn contains_ldmatrix_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1291	    contents.contains("ldmatrix.sync.aligned.")
1292	}
1293	
1294	fn contains_stmatrix_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1295	    contents.contains("stmatrix.sync.aligned.")
1296	}
1297	
1298	/// PTX 8.6 matrix shapes/types have a Blackwell architecture-family floor.
1299	fn contains_blackwell_matrix_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1300	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1301	        let newer_ldmatrix = statement.contains("ldmatrix.sync.aligned.")
1302	            && [".m16n16.", ".m8n16.", ".b8", ".src_fmt", ".dst_fmt"]
1303	                .iter()
1304	                .any(|token| statement.contains(token));
1305	        let newer_stmatrix = statement.contains("stmatrix.sync.aligned.")
1306	            && [".m16n8.", ".b8"]
1307	                .iter()
1308	                .any(|token| statement.contains(token));
1309	        newer_ldmatrix || newer_stmatrix
1310	    })
1311	}
1312	
1313	fn contains_ldmatrix_cta_state_space(contents: &str) -> bool {
1314	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1315	        statement.contains("ldmatrix.sync.aligned.") && statement.contains(".shared::cta.")
1316	    })
1317	}
1318	
1319	/// Checks for features whose minimum target is sm_80.
1320	///
1321	/// This category includes packed bf16 operations introduced on Ampere and
1322	/// non-bulk asynchronous copies. Match both the PTX spellings used in inline
1323	/// assembly and the dotted LLVM NVVM intrinsic names for `cp.async`. Bulk and
1324	/// tensor-copy forms have stronger requirements and are classified first.
1325	fn contains_sm80_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1326	    [
1327	        "fma.rn.bf16x2",
1328	        "fma.rn.relu.bf16x2",
1329	        "min.bf16x2",
1330	        "max.bf16x2",
1331	        "neg.bf16x2",
1332	        "abs.bf16x2",
1333	    ]
1334	    .iter()
1335	    .any(|mnemonic| contains_instruction_mnemonic(contents, mnemonic))
1336	        || contains_mma_m8n8k4_f64_features(contents)
1337	        || contents
1338	            .split(';')
1339	            .any(|statement| statement.contains("cvt.") && statement.contains(".bf16x2.f32"))
1340	        || contains_mbarrier_features(contents)
1341	        || contents.contains("redux.sync")
1342	        || contents.contains("cp.async.ca.shared")
1343	        || contents.contains("cp.async.cg.shared")
1344	        || contents.contains("cp.async.commit_group")
1345	        || contents.contains("cp.async.commit.group")
1346	        || contents.contains("cp.async.wait_group")
1347	        || contents.contains("cp.async.wait.group")
1348	        || contents.contains("cp.async.wait_all")
1349	        || contents.contains("cp.async.wait.all")
1350	        || contains_mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16_features(contents)
1351	}
1352	
1353	/// Checks for TMA/mbarrier instructions (Hopper+ compatible with Blackwell).
1354	///
1355	/// These instructions work on BOTH Hopper and Blackwell:
1356	/// - TMA: Tensor Memory Accelerator bulk copies
1357	/// - mbarrier: Async hardware barriers with transaction tracking
1358	///
1359	/// The architecture floor is generic sm_90; automatic cross-compilation keeps
1360	/// the existing sm_100 default for forward-compatible Blackwell PTX.
1361	fn contains_tma_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1362	    // TMA tensor copies and their commit/wait group controls.
1363	    contains_cp_async_bulk_features(contents)
1364	        || contains_mbarrier_sm90_features(contents)
1365	        || contents.contains("fence.mbarrier_init")
1366	        // Proxy fence for async operations
1367	        || contents.contains("fence.proxy.async")
1368	        || contents.contains(".sync_restrict")
1369	}
1370	
1371	fn contains_cp_async_bulk_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1372	    contents.contains("cp.async.bulk.")
1373	}
1374	
1375	fn contains_mbarrier_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1376	    contents.contains("mbarrier.") || contents.contains("llvm.nvvm.mbarrier")
1377	}
1378	
1379	fn contains_mbarrier_sm90_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1380	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1381	        (statement.contains("mbarrier.") || statement.contains("llvm.nvvm.mbarrier"))
1382	            && [
1383	                "try_wait",
1384	                "expect_tx",
1385	                "complete_tx",
1386	                "shared::cluster",
1387	                ".acquire.",
1388	                ".release.",
1389	                ".relaxed",
1390	            ]
1391	            .iter()
1392	            .any(|feature| statement.contains(feature))
1393	    })
1394	}
1395	
1396	fn contains_mbarrier_ptx71_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1397	    contents
1398	        .split(';')
1399	        .any(|statement| statement.contains("mbarrier.test_wait") && statement.contains(".parity"))
1400	}
1401	
1402	fn contains_mbarrier_ptx78_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1403	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1404	        statement.contains("mbarrier.")
1405	            && (statement.contains("try_wait") || statement.contains("shared::cta"))
1406	    })
1407	}
1408	
1409	fn contains_mbarrier_ptx80_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1410	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1411	        statement.contains("mbarrier.")
1412	            && [
1413	                "expect_tx",
1414	                "complete_tx",
1415	                "shared::cluster",
1416	                ".acquire.",
1417	                ".release.",
1418	            ]
1419	            .iter()
1420	            .any(|feature| statement.contains(feature))
1421	    })
1422	}
1423	
1424	/// Checks for Blackwell tcgen05 instructions (sm_100a+).
1425	///
1426	/// These instructions require a datacenter-Blackwell `a`/`f` target; consumer
1427	/// sm_120 does not provide Tensor Memory:
1428	/// - tcgen05: Tensor Core Gen 5 (TMEM allocation, MMA, sync primitives)
1429	///
1430	/// Key differences from Hopper:
1431	/// - tcgen05 MMA is single-thread (vs WGMMA's 128 threads)
1432	/// - Uses Tensor Memory (TMEM) instead of registers
1433	/// - Different synchronization model (mbarrier-based)
1434	fn contains_blackwell_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1435	    // Keep the instruction-family match broad enough for inline PTX and LLVM
1436	    // intrinsic names, but do not treat debug filenames such as `tcgen05.rs`
1437	    // as an instruction.
1438	    [
1439	        "tcgen05.alloc",
1440	        "tcgen05.dealloc",
1441	        "tcgen05.relinquish_alloc_permit",
1442	        "tcgen05.fence",
1443	        "tcgen05.commit",
1444	        "tcgen05.mma",
1445	        "tcgen05.cp",
1446	        "tcgen05.shift",
1447	        "tcgen05.ld",
1448	        "tcgen05.st",
1449	        "tcgen05.wait",
1450	    ]
1451	    .iter()
1452	    .any(|instruction| contents.contains(instruction))
1453	}
1454	
1455	/// Checks for base TMA multicast in LLVM IR or inline PTX.
1456	///
1457	/// TMA multicast (`cp.async.bulk.tensor...multicast::cluster`) is an optional
1458	/// qualifier that broadcasts a tile to all CTAs in a cluster. It is legal on
1459	/// sm_90+, although NVIDIA advises an `a`/`f` target
1460	/// for performance. In the LLVM intrinsic this is controlled by the trailing
1461	/// `use_cta_mask` i1 argument being set to true.
1462	fn contains_tma_multicast(contents: &str) -> bool {
1463	    contents.lines().any(|line| {
1464	        line.contains("g2s.tile") && (line.contains(", i1 1, i1") || line.contains(", i1 true, i1"))
1465	    }) || contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1466	        statement.contains("cp.async.bulk.tensor") && statement.contains(".multicast::cluster")
1467	    })
1468	}
1469	
1470	/// Checks Blackwell-only TMA forms with an explicit CTA-group qualifier.
1471	fn contains_tma_cta_group_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1472	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1473	        statement.contains("cp.async.bulk.tensor")
1474	            && (statement.contains(".cta_group::1") || statement.contains(".cta_group::2"))
1475	    }) || contents.lines().any(|line| {
1476	        line.contains("g2s.tile") && (line.contains(", i32 1)") || line.contains(", i32 2)"))
1477	    })
1478	}
1479	
1480	/// Checks TMA copies whose destination is CTA-local shared memory.
1481	///
1482	/// `.shared::cta` already existed as a source state space for shared-to-global
1483	/// copies, so the following `.global` source qualifier is part of the match.
1484	/// The destination form was introduced in PTX 8.6 but is valid on sm_90.
1485	fn contains_tma_shared_cta_destination(contents: &str) -> bool {
1486	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1487	        statement.contains("cp.async.bulk.") && statement.contains(".shared::cta.global")
1488	    })
1489	}
1490	
1491	/// Checks PTX 8.6 TMA modifiers with a generic sm_100 architecture floor.
1492	fn contains_tma_sm100_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1493	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1494	        if !statement.contains("cp.async.bulk.") {
1495	            return false;
1496	        }
1497	        statement.contains(".cp_mask")
1498	            || (contains_tma_gather_or_im2col(statement)
1499	                && statement.contains(".shared::cta.global"))
1500	    })
1501	}
1502	
1503	/// Checks PTX 8.6 TMA modes restricted to datacenter Blackwell targets.
1504	fn contains_tma_blackwell_accelerated_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1505	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1506	        if !statement.contains("cp.async.bulk.") {
1507	            return false;
1508	        }
1509	        statement.contains(".tile::scatter4")
1510	            || statement.contains(".im2col::w::128")
1511	            || (contains_tma_gather_or_im2col(statement)
1512	                && !statement.contains(".shared::cta.global"))
1513	    })
1514	}
1515	
1516	fn contains_tma_gather_or_im2col(statement: &str) -> bool {
1517	    statement.contains(".tile::gather4")
1518	        || (statement.contains(".im2col::w") && !statement.contains(".im2col::w::128"))
1519	}
1520	
1521	fn contains_tma_ptx86_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1522	    contains_tma_sm100_features(contents)
1523	        || contains_tma_blackwell_accelerated_features(contents)
1524	        || contents.contains(".sync_restrict")
1525	        || contents
1526	            .split(';')
1527	            .any(|statement| statement.contains("mbarrier.") && statement.contains(".relaxed"))
1528	}
1529	
1530	fn contains_clc_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1531	    contents.contains("clusterlaunchcontrol.")
1532	}
1533	
1534	fn contains_clc_multicast_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1535	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1536	        statement.contains("clusterlaunchcontrol.")
1537	            && statement.contains(".multicast::cluster::all")
1538	    })
1539	}
1540	
1541	fn contains_cluster_ptx80_features(contents: &str) -> bool {
1542	    contents.split(';').any(|statement| {
1543	        statement.contains("barrier.cluster.")
1544	            && [".release", ".relaxed", ".acquire"]
1545	                .iter()
1546	                .any(|qualifier| statement.contains(qualifier))
1547	    })
1548	}
1549	
1550	/// GPU feature requirements detected in one LLVM module.
1551	///
1552	/// This is a set rather than a single "strongest" feature: architecture
1553	/// families are not totally ordered. For example, WGMMA requires Hopper
1554	/// `sm_90a`, while PTX 8.6 matrix forms require Blackwell. Keeping every bit
1555	/// lets target validation enforce the intersection instead of silently
1556	/// choosing whichever instruction happened to have higher detector priority.
1557	#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
1558	struct DetectedFeatures(u32);
1559	
1560	#[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
1561	impl DetectedFeatures {
1562	    /// tcgen05/TMEM (Blackwell datacenter, sm_100a).
1563	    const Blackwell: Self = Self(1 << 0);
1564	    /// Base TMA multicast (sm_90+, with architecture/family targets preferred).
1565	    const TmaMulticast: Self = Self(1 << 1);
1566	    /// Explicit CTA-group TMA forms (Blackwell datacenter family).
1567	    const TmaCtaGroup: Self = Self(1 << 2);
1568	    /// PTX 8.6 ldmatrix/stmatrix shapes supported on Blackwell family targets.
1569	    const MatrixBlackwell: Self = Self(1 << 3);
1570	    /// WGMMA (Hopper only, sm_90a - NOT forward-compatible).
1571	    const Wgmma: Self = Self(1 << 4);
1572	    /// TMA/mbarrier (Hopper+ compatible).
1573	    const Tma: Self = Self(1 << 5);
1574	    /// Thread Block Clusters (sm_90+, forward-compatible).
1575	    const Cluster: Self = Self(1 << 6);
1576	    /// Forward-compatible instructions with an sm_90 floor.
1577	    const Sm90: Self = Self(1 << 7);
1578	    /// Forward-compatible instructions with an sm_80 floor.
1579	    const Sm80: Self = Self(1 << 8);
1580	    /// Warp matrix register transpose introduced in PTX 7.8 on sm_75.
1581	    const Movmatrix: Self = Self(1 << 9);
1582	    /// Warp matrix shared-memory load introduced in PTX 6.5 on sm_75.
1583	    const Ldmatrix: Self = Self(1 << 10);
1584	    /// No special features (Volta+, with an sm_80 cross-compile default).
1585	    const Basic: Self = Self(1 << 11);
1586	    /// Generic Blackwell-or-newer operations such as base CLC and TMA cp_mask.
1587	    const Sm100: Self = Self(1 << 12);
1588	    /// Architecture/family-specific Blackwell features also available on consumers.
1589	    const BlackwellFamily: Self = Self(1 << 13);
1590	    /// Architecture/family-specific datacenter Blackwell TMA modes.
1591	    const BlackwellAccelerated: Self = Self(1 << 14);
1592	    /// Floating-point `redux.sync` (the sm_100/sm_103 architecture family).
1593	    const ReduxF32: Self = Self(1 << 15);
1594	    /// FP8 / f16-accumulator multimem forms on supported Blackwell families.
1595	    const MultimemFp8: Self = Self(1 << 16);
1596	
1597	    const ALL: [Self; 17] = [
1598	        Self::Blackwell,
1599	        Self::TmaCtaGroup,
1600	        Self::BlackwellAccelerated,
1601	        Self::BlackwellFamily,
1602	        Self::ReduxF32,
1603	        Self::MultimemFp8,
1604	        Self::TmaMulticast,
1605	        Self::MatrixBlackwell,
1606	        Self::Wgmma,
1607	        Self::Tma,
1608	        Self::Cluster,
1609	        Self::Sm90,
1610	        Self::Sm80,
1611	        Self::Movmatrix,
1612	        Self::Ldmatrix,
1613	        Self::Sm100,
1614	        Self::Basic,
1615	    ];
1616	
1617	    const fn empty() -> Self {
1618	        Self(0)
1619	    }
1620	
1621	    const fn contains(self, feature: Self) -> bool {
1622	        self.0 & feature.0 != 0
1623	    }
1624	
1625	    fn insert(&mut self, feature: Self) {
1626	        self.0 |= feature.0;
1627	    }
1628	
1629	    fn iter(self) -> impl Iterator<Item = Self> {
1630	        Self::ALL
1631	            .into_iter()
1632	            .filter(move |feature| self.contains(*feature))
1633	    }
1634	
1635	    fn name(self) -> &'static str {
1636	        match self {
1637	            Self::Blackwell => "Blackwell",
1638	            Self::TmaMulticast => "TmaMulticast",
1639	            Self::TmaCtaGroup => "TmaCtaGroup",
1640	            Self::MatrixBlackwell => "MatrixBlackwell",
1641	            Self::Wgmma => "Wgmma",
1642	            Self::Tma => "Tma",
1643	            Self::Cluster => "Cluster",
1644	            Self::Sm90 => "Sm90",
1645	            Self::Sm80 => "Sm80",
1646	            Self::Movmatrix => "Movmatrix",
1647	            Self::Ldmatrix => "Ldmatrix",
1648	            Self::Sm100 => "Sm100",
1649	            Self::BlackwellFamily => "BlackwellFamily",
1650	            Self::BlackwellAccelerated => "BlackwellAccelerated",
1651	            Self::ReduxF32 => "ReduxF32",
1652	            Self::MultimemFp8 => "MultimemFp8",
1653	            Self::Basic => "Basic",
1654	            _ => "Unknown",
1655	        }
1656	    }
1657	}
1658	
1659	impl std::fmt::Debug for DetectedFeatures {
1660	    fn fmt(&self, formatter: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
1661	        let mut first = true;
1662	        for feature in self.iter() {
1663	            if !first {
1664	                formatter.write_str(" + ")?;
1665	            }
1666	            formatter.write_str(feature.name())?;
1667	            first = false;
1668	        }
1669	        Ok(())
1670	    }
1671	}
1672	
1673	impl std::ops::BitOr for DetectedFeatures {
1674	    type Output = Self;
1675	
1676	    fn bitor(self, rhs: Self) -> Self::Output {
1677	        Self(self.0 | rhs.0)
1678	    }
1679	}
1680	
1681	/// PTX ISA requirements are independent of the GPU architecture floor.
1682	///
1683	/// For example, a module may need sm_80 because it uses `cp.async` and still
1684	/// need PTX 7.8 because it also uses `movmatrix`.
1685	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
1686	enum PtxIsaRequirement {
1687	    Default,
1688	    Ptx62,
1689	    Ptx65,
1690	    Ptx70,
1691	    Ptx71,
1692	    Ptx78,
1693	    Ptx80,
1694	    Ptx86,
1695	}
1696	
1697	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
1698	struct ModuleRequirements {
1699	    features: DetectedFeatures,
1700	    ptx_isa: PtxIsaRequirement,
1701	}
1702	
1703	/// Detect every architecture requirement in exported LLVM text.
1704	///
1705	/// Both the ordinary PTX path and automatic libdevice mode use this exact
1706	/// detector. The latter renders an in-memory preview before choosing the NVVM
1707	/// pointer dialect.
1708	fn detect_features_in_llvm_text(contents: &str) -> DetectedFeatures {
1709	    let mut features = DetectedFeatures::empty();
1710	    for (present, feature) in [
1711	        (
1712	            contains_blackwell_features(contents),
1713	            DetectedFeatures::Blackwell,
1714	        ),
1715	        (
1716	            contains_tma_cta_group_features(contents),
1717	            DetectedFeatures::TmaCtaGroup,
1718	        ),
1719	        (
1720	            contains_tma_blackwell_accelerated_features(contents),
1721	            DetectedFeatures::BlackwellAccelerated,
1722	        ),
1723	        (
1724	            contains_clc_multicast_features(contents),
1725	            DetectedFeatures::BlackwellFamily,
1726	        ),
1727	        (
1728	            contains_redux_f32_features(contents),
1729	            DetectedFeatures::ReduxF32,
1730	        ),
1731	        (
1732	            contains_multimem_blackwell_features(contents),
1733	            DetectedFeatures::MultimemFp8,
1734	        ),
1735	        (
1736	            contains_tma_multicast(contents),
1737	            DetectedFeatures::TmaMulticast,
1738	        ),
1739	        (
1740	            contains_blackwell_matrix_features(contents),
1741	            DetectedFeatures::MatrixBlackwell,
1742	        ),
1743	        (contains_wgmma_features(contents), DetectedFeatures::Wgmma),
1744	        (contains_tma_features(contents), DetectedFeatures::Tma),
1745	        (
1746	            contains_cluster_features(contents),
1747	            DetectedFeatures::Cluster,
1748	        ),
1749	        (contains_sm90_features(contents), DetectedFeatures::Sm90),
1750	        (contains_sm80_features(contents), DetectedFeatures::Sm80),
1751	        (
1752	            contains_movmatrix_features(contents),
1753	            DetectedFeatures::Movmatrix,
1754	        ),
1755	        (
1756	            contains_ldmatrix_features(contents),
1757	            DetectedFeatures::Ldmatrix,
1758	        ),
1759	        (
1760	            contains_tma_sm100_features(contents) || contains_clc_features(contents),
1761	            DetectedFeatures::Sm100,
1762	        ),
1763	    ] {
1764	        if present {
1765	            features.insert(feature);
1766	        }
1767	    }
1768	    if features == DetectedFeatures::empty() {
1769	        features.insert(DetectedFeatures::Basic);
1770	    }
1771	    features
1772	}
1773	
1774	fn detect_module_requirements_in_llvm_text(contents: &str) -> ModuleRequirements {
1775	    let mut ptx_isa = PtxIsaRequirement::Default;
1776	    if contains_packed_f16_atomic_features(contents) {
1777	        ptx_isa = ptx_isa.max(PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx62);
1778	    }
1779	    if contains_ldmatrix_features(contents) {
1780	        ptx_isa = ptx_isa.max(PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx65);
1781	    }
1782	    if contains_mbarrier_features(contents)
1783	        || contents.contains("redux.sync")
1784	        || contains_mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16_features(contents)
1785	        || contains_mma_m8n8k4_f64_features(contents)
1786	    {
1787	        ptx_isa = ptx_isa.max(PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx70);
1788	    }
1789	    if contains_mbarrier_ptx71_features(contents) {
1790	        ptx_isa = ptx_isa.max(PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx71);
1791	    }
1792	    if contains_movmatrix_features(contents)
1793	        || contains_stmatrix_features(contents)
1794	        || contains_ldmatrix_cta_state_space(contents)
1795	        || contains_cluster_features(contents)
1796	        || contains_mbarrier_ptx78_features(contents)
1797	        || contains_packed_bf16_atomic_features(contents)
1798	    {
1799	        ptx_isa = ptx_isa.max(PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx78);
1800	    }
1801	    if contains_cp_async_bulk_features(contents)
1802	        || contains_wgmma_features(contents)
1803	        || contains_cluster_ptx80_features(contents)
1804	        || contains_elect_features(contents)
1805	        || contains_mbarrier_ptx80_features(contents)
1806	        || contents.contains("fence.mbarrier_init")
1807	        || contents.contains("fence.proxy.async")
1808	    {
1809	        ptx_isa = ptx_isa.max(PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx80);
1810	    }
1811	    if contains_blackwell_matrix_features(contents)
1812	        || contains_tma_cta_group_features(contents)
1813	        || contains_tma_shared_cta_destination(contents)
1814	        || contains_tma_ptx86_features(contents)
1815	        || contains_clc_features(contents)
1816	        || contains_blackwell_features(contents)
1817	        || contains_fence_acquire_release_features(contents)
1818	        || contains_multimem_features(contents)
1819	        || contains_redux_f32_features(contents)
1820	    {
1821	        ptx_isa = ptx_isa.max(PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx86);
1822	    }
1823	
1824	    ModuleRequirements {
1825	        features: detect_features_in_llvm_text(contents),
1826	        ptx_isa,
1827	    }
1828	}
1829	
1830	fn detect_module_requirements_in_llvm_file(
1831	    ll_path: &Path,
1832	) -> Result<ModuleRequirements, PipelineError> {
1833	    let contents = std::fs::read_to_string(ll_path).map_err(|error| {
1834	        PipelineError::PtxGeneration(format!(
1835	            "failed to inspect generated LLVM IR {}: {error}",
1836	            ll_path.display()
1837	        ))
1838	    })?;
1839	    Ok(detect_module_requirements_in_llvm_text(&contents))
1840	}
1841	
1842	/// Select a concrete architecture that satisfies every detected feature.
1843	///
1844	/// The first candidate preserves the established default for a module's most
1845	/// restrictive-looking feature. The remaining candidates handle intersections
1846	/// such as WGMMA + TMA multicast, whose only common target is `sm_90a`.
1847	fn select_target(features: DetectedFeatures) -> Result<&'static str, String> {
1848	    let preferred = if features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Blackwell)
1849	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::TmaCtaGroup)
1850	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::BlackwellAccelerated)
1851	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::BlackwellFamily)
1852	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::ReduxF32)
1853	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::MultimemFp8)
1854	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::TmaMulticast)
1855	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::MatrixBlackwell)
1856	    {
1857	        "sm_100a"
1858	    } else if features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Wgmma) {
1859	        "sm_90a"
1860	    } else if features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Sm100) {
1861	        "sm_100"
1862	    } else if features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Tma) {
1863	        // Plain TMA is compatible with Hopper, but sm_100 is the existing
1864	        // cross-compilation default because it produces forward-compatible
1865	        // PTX for generic Blackwell devices.
1866	        "sm_100"
1867	    } else if features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Cluster)
1868	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Sm90)
1869	    {
1870	        "sm_90"
1871	    } else if features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Sm80) {
1872	        "sm_80"
1873	    } else if features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Movmatrix)
1874	        || features.contains(DetectedFeatures::Ldmatrix)
1875	    {
1876	        "sm_75"
1877	    } else {
1878	        "sm_80"
1879	    };
1880	
1881	    for candidate in [
1882	        preferred, "sm_100a", "sm_90a", "sm_100", "sm_90", "sm_80", "sm_75",
1883	    ] {
1884	        if arch_satisfies(candidate, features) {
1885	            return Ok(candidate);
1886	        }
1887	    }
1888	
1889	    Err(format!(
1890	        "detected CUDA features {features:?} do not share a compatible GPU architecture"
1891	    ))
1892	}
1893	
1894	/// Does `arch` (e.g. `"sm_120a"`, `"sm_90"`) support the kernel's detected
1895	/// features?
1896	///
1897	/// tcgen05/TMEM and explicit `cta_group` TMA forms exist only in the sm_100
1898	/// datacenter-Blackwell family: consumer Blackwell (sm_120) and Hopper (sm_90)
1899	/// lack them, so an sm_120 GPU cannot run an sm_100 tcgen05 kernel even though
1900	/// 120 > 100. WGMMA is Hopper-only. The remaining features are forward
1901	/// compatible from their floor (TMA / cluster / sm_90 features need sm_90+,
1902	/// sm_80 features need sm_80+, and basic needs sm_70+).
1903	///
1904	/// Used to decide whether the GPU in this machine (the `CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_ARCH`
1905	/// hint) can actually run the kernel, or whether we must build for the arch the
1906	/// IR requires instead.
1907	fn arch_satisfies(arch: &str, features: DetectedFeatures) -> bool {
1908	    let Some((capability, suffix)) = arch_compute_capability_and_suffix(arch) else {
1909	        return false;
1910	    };
1911	    if !is_known_cuda_target(capability, suffix) {
1912	        return false;
1913	    }
1914	    features
1915	        .iter()
1916	        .all(|feature| arch_satisfies_feature(capability, suffix, feature))
1917	}
1918	
1919	fn arch_satisfies_feature(
1920	    capability: u32,
1921	    suffix: Option<char>,
1922	    feature: DetectedFeatures,
1923	) -> bool {
1924	    let major = capability / 10;
1925	    match feature {
1926	        DetectedFeatures::Blackwell | DetectedFeatures::TmaCtaGroup => {
1927	            supports_tcgen_target(capability, suffix)
1928	        }
1929	        DetectedFeatures::BlackwellAccelerated => {
1930	            supports_blackwell_accelerated_target(capability, suffix)
1931	        }
1932	        DetectedFeatures::BlackwellFamily | DetectedFeatures::MatrixBlackwell => {
1933	            supports_blackwell_family_target(capability, suffix)
1934	        }
1935	        DetectedFeatures::ReduxF32 => supports_redux_f32_target(capability, suffix),
1936	        DetectedFeatures::MultimemFp8 => supports_multimem_fp8_target(capability, suffix),
1937	        // The PTX ISA requires only sm_90+. The suffixed targets are advised
1938	        // for performance, so target selection still prefers sm_100a.
1939	        DetectedFeatures::TmaMulticast => major >= 9,
1940	        DetectedFeatures::Wgmma => capability == 90 && suffix == Some('a'),
1941	        DetectedFeatures::Sm100 => is_known_blackwell_capability(capability),
1942	        DetectedFeatures::Tma | DetectedFeatures::Cluster | DetectedFeatures::Sm90 => major >= 9,
1943	        DetectedFeatures::Sm80 => major >= 8,
1944	        DetectedFeatures::Movmatrix | DetectedFeatures::Ldmatrix => capability >= 75,
1945	        // Basic kernels are supported on the project's Volta+ floor. The
1946	        // cross-compilation default remains sm_80, but a detected sm_70/sm_75
1947	        // GPU is a valid and more useful target for `cargo oxide run`.
1948	        DetectedFeatures::Basic => major >= 7,
1949	        // `iter` only yields the single-bit constants above.
1950	        _ => false,
1951	    }
1952	}
1953	
1954	/// tcgen05/TMEM exists only on the datacenter Blackwell architecture or
1955	/// family targets. Consumer sm_120 and generic targets without an `a`/`f`
1956	/// suffix do not provide Tensor Memory.
1957	fn supports_tcgen_target(capability: u32, suffix: Option<char>) -> bool {
1958	    match suffix {
1959	        // Architecture-specific targets are exact, not numerically forward
1960	        // compatible. `sm_101a` is the PTX 8.x spelling later renamed to
1961	        // `sm_110a`; accept both spellings plus the distinct sm_103 target.
1962	        Some('a') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110),
1963	        Some('f') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110),
1964	        _ => false,
1965	    }
1966	}
1967	
1968	fn supports_blackwell_accelerated_target(capability: u32, suffix: Option<char>) -> bool {
1969	    match suffix {
1970	        Some('a') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110),
1971	        Some('f') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110),
1972	        _ => false,
1973	    }
1974	}
1975	
1976	fn supports_blackwell_family_target(capability: u32, suffix: Option<char>) -> bool {
1977	    match suffix {
1978	        Some('a') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 110 | 120),
1979	        Some('f') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110 | 120 | 121),
1980	        _ => false,
1981	    }
1982	}
1983	
1984	/// Floating-point `redux.sync` is scoped to the sm_100/sm_103 family.
1985	fn supports_redux_f32_target(capability: u32, suffix: Option<char>) -> bool {
1986	    matches!(suffix, Some('a' | 'f')) && matches!(capability, 100 | 103)
1987	}
1988	
1989	/// FP8 / f16-accumulator multimem forms span several Blackwell architecture
1990	/// targets, but consumer family (`f`) targets do not support the sm_120 line.
1991	fn supports_multimem_fp8_target(capability: u32, suffix: Option<char>) -> bool {
1992	    match suffix {
1993	        Some('a') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110 | 120 | 121),
1994	        Some('f') => matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110),
1995	        _ => false,
1996	    }
1997	}
1998	
1999	fn is_known_blackwell_capability(capability: u32) -> bool {
2000	    matches!(capability, 100 | 101 | 103 | 110 | 120 | 121)
2001	}
2002	
2003	fn is_known_cuda_target(capability: u32, suffix: Option<char>) -> bool {
2004	    let known_capability = matches!(
2005	        capability,
2006	        70 | 72 | 75 | 80 | 86 | 87 | 88 | 89 | 90 | 100 | 101 | 103 | 110 | 120 | 121
2007	    );
2008	    known_capability
2009	        && match suffix {
2010	            None => true,
2011	            Some('a') => capability == 90 || is_known_blackwell_capability(capability),
2012	            Some('f') => is_known_blackwell_capability(capability),
2013	            _ => false,
2014	        }
2015	}
2016	
2017	fn validate_target_features(target: &CudaArch, features: DetectedFeatures) -> Result<(), String> {
2018	    let compatible_default = select_target(features)?;
2019	    if arch_satisfies(&target.sm(), features) {
2020	        return Ok(());
2021	    }
2022	
2023	    Err(format!(
2024	        "CUDA target {} cannot lower detected feature {features:?}; \
2025	         cuda-oxide requires a target compatible with {} for this module",
2026	        target.sm(),
2027	        compatible_default
2028	    ))
2029	}
2030	
2031	fn resolve_ptx_target(
2032	    explicit_override: Option<&str>,
2033	    device_hint: Option<&str>,
2034	    detected: DetectedFeatures,
2035	) -> Result<(String, &'static str), PipelineError> {
2036	    if let Some(target) = explicit_override {
2037	        let parsed = target.parse::<CudaArch>().map_err(|error| {
2038	            PipelineError::PtxGeneration(format!("invalid CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET `{target}`: {error}"))
2039	        })?;
2040	        validate_target_features(&parsed, detected).map_err(PipelineError::PtxGeneration)?;
2041	        return Ok((parsed.sm(), "CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET"));
2042	    }
2043	
2044	    if let Some(device) = device_hint.filter(|target| arch_satisfies(target, detected)) {
2045	        return Ok((device.to_string(), "detected GPU"));
2046	    }
2047	
2048	    let target = select_target(detected).map_err(PipelineError::PtxGeneration)?;
2049	    Ok((target.to_string(), "feature requirement"))
2050	}
2051	
2052	/// Select the PTX ISA independently from the GPU architecture.
2053	///
2054	/// LLVM GPU CPUs select a default PTX ISA independently from the hardware
2055	/// feature floor. Raise that ISA only when the selected CPU's default is too
2056	/// old; never force a newer target back to an older PTX version.
2057	fn required_ptx_feature(target: &str, requirement: PtxIsaRequirement) -> Option<&'static str> {
2058	    let capability = arch_compute_capability(target)?;
2059	    let minimum = target_minimum_ptx_isa(capability)?;
2060	    let requested = match requirement {
2061	        PtxIsaRequirement::Default => return None,
2062	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx62 => 62,
2063	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx65 => 65,
2064	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx70 => 70,
2065	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx71 => 71,
2066	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx78 => 78,
2067	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx80 => 80,
2068	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx86 => 86,
2069	    };
2070	    if requested <= minimum {
2071	        return None;
2072	    }
2073	    match requirement {
2074	        PtxIsaRequirement::Default => None,
2075	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx62 => Some("+ptx62"),
2076	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx65 => Some("+ptx65"),
2077	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx70 => Some("+ptx70"),
2078	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx71 => Some("+ptx71"),
2079	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx78 => Some("+ptx78"),
2080	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx80 => Some("+ptx80"),
2081	        PtxIsaRequirement::Ptx86 => Some("+ptx86"),
2082	    }
2083	}
2084	
2085	/// Minimum PTX ISA accepted by LLVM for each concrete target. Passing an
2086	/// older `+ptxNN` feature does not merely do nothing: LLVM aborts because that
2087	/// ISA cannot name the selected processor.
2088	fn target_minimum_ptx_isa(capability: u32) -> Option<u32> {
2089	    match capability {
2090	        70 => Some(60),
2091	        72 => Some(61),
2092	        75 => Some(63),
2093	        80 => Some(70),
2094	        86 => Some(71),
2095	        87 => Some(74),
2096	        88 => Some(90),
2097	        89 | 90 => Some(78),
2098	        100 | 101 => Some(86),
2099	        103 => Some(88),
2100	        110 => Some(90),
2101	        120 => Some(87),
2102	        121 => Some(88),
2103	        _ => None,
2104	    }
2105	}
2106	
2107	/// Reject targets that the supported LLVM 21 backend silently mishandles.
2108	///
2109	/// LLVM 21 accepts `-mcpu=sm_88` / `sm_110*` but only prints a warning and
2110	/// emits PTX 6.0, which ptxas then rejects. LLVM 22 is the first backend in
2111	/// cuda-oxide's supported toolchain set that emits valid PTX for these PTX 9.0
2112	/// target spellings. An unknown version is rejected because it cannot prove
2113	/// that the backend knows the processor.
2114	fn validate_target_for_llvm_major(target: &str, llc_major: Option<u32>) -> Result<(), String> {
2115	    let capability = arch_compute_capability(target);
2116	    if matches!(capability, Some(88 | 110)) && llc_major.is_none_or(|major| major < 22) {
2117	        let backend = llc_major.map_or_else(
2118	            || "an LLVM backend with an unknown version".to_string(),
2119	            |major| format!("LLVM {major}"),
2120	        );
2121	        return Err(format!(
2122	            "CUDA target {target} requires LLVM 22 or newer; {backend} does not reliably emit valid PTX for this PTX 9.0 target"
2123	        ));
2124	    }
2125	    Ok(())
2126	}
2127	
2128	/// Extract the compute-capability *major* version from an `sm_…` target string.
2129	///
2130	/// CUDA concatenates major+minor without a separator, so `"sm_120a"` is cc 12.0
2131	/// (major 12), `"sm_90"` is cc 9.0, `"sm_103a"` is cc 10.3. We read the digit
2132	/// run after `sm_` and divide by ten. Returns `None` when there are no digits.
2133	#[cfg(test)]
2134	fn arch_major(arch: &str) -> Option<u32> {
2135	    arch_compute_capability(arch).map(|capability| capability / 10)
2136	}
2137	
2138	/// Extract the numeric compute capability from an `sm_…` target.
2139	fn arch_compute_capability(arch: &str) -> Option<u32> {
2140	    arch_compute_capability_and_suffix(arch).map(|(capability, _)| capability)
2141	}

6	//! Basic block translation: MIR block → Pliron IR block contents.
7	//!
8	//! Translates the contents of a single MIR basic block (statements + terminator)
9	//! into Pliron IR operations. The block itself is created by [`super::body`];
10	//! this module just populates it.
11	//!
12	//! # Translation order
13	//!
14	//! 1. Statements are translated in order, emitting loads/stores against the
15	//!    per-local alloca slots recorded in [`ValueMap`].
16	//! 2. Terminator is translated last and emits zero-operand control-flow ops
17	//!    (`mir.goto`, `mir.cond_br`, `mir.switch`, `mir.return`). Cross-block
18	//!    data flow happens via the slots, not block arguments.
19	
20	use super::statement;
21	use super::terminator;
22	use crate::error::TranslationResult;
23	use crate::translator::values::ValueMap;
24	use pliron::basic_block::BasicBlock;
25	use pliron::context::{Context, Ptr};
26	use pliron::identifier::Legaliser;
27	use pliron::operation::Operation;
28	use rustc_public::mir;
29	
30	/// Translates a MIR basic block's contents into the corresponding Pliron IR block.
31	///
32	/// # Arguments
33	///
34	/// * `ctx` - Pliron IR context
35	/// * `body` - The full MIR body (needed for local declarations)
36	/// * `mir_block` - The MIR block to translate
37	/// * `_idx` - Block index (unused, kept for debugging)
38	/// * `block_ptr` - Target Pliron IR block (already created)
39	/// * `value_map` - MIR local → alloca slot mapping
40	/// * `block_map` - Block index → Pliron IR block mapping
41	/// * `legaliser` - Shared identifier legaliser for name uniqueness
42	/// * `entry_prev_op` - For the entry block only: the last op emitted by
43	///   `body::translate_body`'s alloca/store setup (see `emit_entry_allocas`),
44	///   so that statements are appended **after** that setup instead of being
45	///   inserted at the front. For every other block this must be `None`.
46	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
47	pub fn translate_block(
48	    ctx: &mut Context,
49	    body: &mir::Body,
50	    mir_block: &mir::BasicBlock,
51	    _idx: usize,
52	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
53	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
54	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
55	    legaliser: &mut Legaliser,
56	    entry_prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
57	) -> TranslationResult<()> {
58	    let mut prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>> = entry_prev_op;
59	    for stmt in &mir_block.statements {
60	        let op_ptr =
61	            statement::translate_statement(ctx, body, stmt, value_map, block_ptr, prev_op)?;
62	        prev_op = op_ptr;
63	    }
64	
65	    let _term_op_ptr = terminator::translate_terminator(
66	        ctx,
67	        body,
68	        &mir_block.terminator,
69	        value_map,
70	        block_ptr,
71	        prev_op,
72	        block_map,
73	        legaliser,
74	    )?;
75	
76	    Ok(())
77	}

# MIR 导入器鸟瞰：rustc MIR → Pliron IR

## 1. 本讲目标

上一讲（u4-l1）我们停在后端入口 `codegen_crate` 的 host/device 分流，并追到 `device_codegen::generate_device_code` 把控制权交给 `mir_importer::run_pipeline` 这一步。本讲就跨过这道门，鸟瞰 `mir-importer` crate 内部发生的事。

读完本讲，你应该能够：

1. 说出 `mir-importer` 在 cuda-oxide 流水线里的定位——把 rustc 的 **MIR** 翻译成基于 **Pliron** 的 `dialect-mir` IR，再驱动后续的 `mem2reg`、lowering、导出 PTX。
2. 画出 `run_pipeline` 的阶段序列，并指出 `translator` 只负责其中「MIR → dialect-mir」这一段。
3. 解释 `translator` 的**分层结构**（body / block / statement / rvalue / terminator / values / types）以及贯穿全层的 **alloca + load/store 模型**。
4. 说明一个 rustc MIR 的「基本块」是如何被映射成一串 Pliron IR 块、unwind cleanup 块又是如何被处理的。

本讲是「鸟瞰」（overview）性质：只讲清楚 `mir-importer` 的骨架与数据流，不深入单条 MIR 语句如何翻译成具体 op。对 terminator/intrinsic 的逐类翻译、对 lowering 细节的深潜，分别留给 u6-l2（mir-importer 深潜）与 u6-l3（mir-lower 深潜）。

## 2. 前置知识

### 2.1 什么是 MIR

rustc 把 Rust 源码经过 AST、HIR，最终降到 **MIR（Mid-level IR，中级中间表示）**。MIR 是一个**有向的控制流图**：

- **基本块（basic block, bb）**：一段顺序执行的语句，末尾挂一个**终止符（terminator）**决定下一个去哪个块。
- **语句（statement）**：主要是 `Assign(place, rvalue)`，即「把右值算出来，写到左值位置上」。
- **终止符（terminator）**：控制流转移，如 `Goto`、`Return`、`Call`、`SwitchInt`。
- **局部变量（local）**：MIR 用编号的局部（`_0` 是返回值，`_1..` 是参数与临时量）传递数据。

MIR 已经做完了借用检查、类型推导，是 rustc 最「接近后端」的、仍然保留 Rust 语义的表示。cuda-oxide 的 device 路径就**从 MIR 接手**，不再回头碰 AST。

### 2.2 什么是 Pliron / dialect

[Pliron](https://github.com/mbartling/pliron) 是一个用 Rust 写的、**MLIR 风格**的 IR 框架。和 MLIR 一样，它的核心思想是**方言（dialect）**：

- 一个 IR 里可以同时存在多个方言，每个方言自带一组 **op（操作）** 和 **类型（type）**。
- cuda-oxide 定义了两个自有方言：`dialect-mir`（高层、贴近 Rust 语义，如 `mir.add`、`mir.store`、`mir.func`）和 `dialect-nvvm`（贴近 PTX/NVVM，如 `warp`、`tma`、`atomic`）。后者会在 u4-l3 详讲。
- Pliron 提供的容器是 **`Context`**（全局 IR 上下文）、**`Operation`**（一个 op 节点）、**`BasicBlock`**（基本块）、**`Region`**（被 op 持有的区域，如函数体）。

如果你用过 MLIR，可以把 `dialect-mir` 类比成 MLIR 里自定义的 dialect，把 `mir.func`/`mir.add` 类比成 `func.func`/`arith.addi`。如果没用过也不必担心——本讲只把它当成「一种带类型的、可打印的 IR 文本」即可。

### 2.3 为什么不直接 MIR → LLVM IR

你可能会问：既然最终要得到 LLVM IR 再生成 PTX，为什么不直接把 MIR 翻译成 LLVM IR？原因在于**分层解耦**：

- MIR 仍然带着 Rust 的高级语义（枚举判别式、变长切片、地址空间未定的引用），一步到位降到 LLVM 会非常臃肿、难维护。
- `dialect-mir` 作为中间层，既保留 Rust 语义、又贴近 IR 框架，方便插入 **`mem2reg`、循环展开**等 pass（见 pipeline.rs 里的步骤）。
- 真正降到 LLVM 方言的工作由 `mir-lower` crate 完成（u4-l4 / u6-l3），与 `mir-importer` 解耦。

理解了这一点，你就理解了 `mir-importer` 的边界：**它只负责 MIR → dialect-mir，及其后的几个 IR 变换；lowering 之后的事交给别的 crate。**

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 Pliron IR 载体：rustc MIR 之外的第二种 IR

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

cuda-oxide 在 device 路径上其实同时处理**两种** IR：

1. **rustc 的 MIR**：输入，由 rustc 内部数据结构（`rustc_middle::mir`）承载。但 `mir-importer` **不直接**啃 rustc 内部结构，而是经 `stable_mir`（`rustc_public` / `rustc_public_bridge`）拿到一份「稳定的、跨版本相对稳定」的 MIR 视图。
2. **Pliron 的 `dialect-mir`**：输出，由 `pliron::context::Context` 承载。translator 的工作就是把前者逐节点搬进后者。

为什么需要这一层？因为后续的 `mem2reg`、`lowering` 都建立在 Pliron 的 pass 框架之上——它们只认 Pliron 的 `Operation`/`BasicBlock`，不认 rustc 的 MIR。所以 `dialect-mir` 是「给下游 pass 看的 Rust 语义 IR」。

crate 顶部的文档注释把这件事画成一张图，读它最快建立直觉。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

```text
rustc 内部 MIR (rustc_middle)
        │  rustc_internal::stable()  ← 由 u4-l1 的 device_codegen 完成
        ▼
stable MIR (rustc_public)         ← mir-importer 实际消费的形态
        │  translator::translate_function
        ▼
Pliron Context 持有 dialect-mir   ← 下游 pass 的输入
        │  verify → mem2reg → unroll
        ▼
mir-lower: dialect-mir → LLVM dialect → .ll → PTX
```

注意图中 `mir-importer` 的产出（Pliron `Context`）**并不落盘成独立的 dialect-mir 文件**——它存在内存里，被 `run_pipeline` 直接喂给后续 pass。能「看到」它的途径只有打开 dump（见 4.4 的实践）。

#### 4.1.3 源码精读

crate 顶层文档注释给出了整体架构与模块分工，是本讲最好的总览入口：

[crates/mir-importer/src/lib.rs:18-46](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/lib.rs#L18-L46) —— 注意 ASCII 图把 `translator` 与 `pipeline` 分开：translator 负责 `MIR → dialect-mir (alloca)`，pipeline 负责 `mem2reg → unroll → LLVM dialect → LLVM IR → PTX`。这是 cuda-oxide 全局最重要的职责切分。

紧接着的文档说明了贯穿 translator 的核心建模：

[crates/mir-importer/src/lib.rs:63-70](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/lib.rs#L63-L70) —— **alloca + load/store 模型**：每个非 ZST 的 MIR local 都物化成一个 `mir.alloca`，写为 `mir.store`、读为 `mir.load`，跨块数据流走槽位而非块参数；`mem2reg` 之后再把标量槽位提升回 SSA。这个模型会在 4.3 重点展开。

crate 对外导出的「门面」类型（下游 `device_codegen` 只用这几个）：

[crates/mir-importer/src/lib.rs:86-90](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/lib.rs#L86-L90) —— `run_pipeline`、`PipelineConfig`、`CollectedFunction`、`CompilationResult` 等。`CollectedFunction` 是输入（已收集、已单态化的设备函数实例），`CompilationResult` 是输出（产物路径、目标架构、FMA 策略）。

#### 4.1.4 代码实践

这是一个纯阅读型实践，目标是把「两种 IR」的边界看清楚。

1. **实践目标**：确认 `mir-importer` 消费的是 stable MIR、产出的是 Pliron `Context`，并定位两者之间的桥接点。
2. **操作步骤**：
   - 打开 `crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs`，看顶部的 `extern crate rustc_public;` 与 `extern crate rustc_public_bridge;`（[mod.rs:72-79](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/lib.rs#L72-L79) 区域），确认它依赖的是 stable MIR 而非 rustc 内部类型。
   - 在 `translate_function` 签名里找到参数 `body: &mir::Body`（来自 `rustc_public::mir`）与返回值 `Ptr<Operation>`（Pliron 操作指针），见 [translator/mod.rs:90-96](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs#L90-L96)。
3. **需要观察的现象**：函数签名「左侧是 stable MIR、右侧是 Pliron」正是「翻译器」一词的字面体现。
4. **预期结果**：你能用一句话说出「`translate_function` 把一个 `rustc_public::mir::Body` 变成一个 Pliron `Operation`」。
5. 「待本地验证」：本实践无需运行任何命令。

#### 4.1.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：为什么 `mir-importer` 通过 `rustc_public`（stable MIR）而不是直接用 `rustc_middle::mir`？

**参考答案**：stable MIR 是 rustc 提供的、跨 nightly 版本相对稳定的接口，能把 translator 与 rustc 内部数据结构的频繁变动隔离开；同时 `#![feature(rustc_private)]` 已经让 crate 能访问内部类型，但「能访问」不等于「应该依赖」。stable MIR 是为这类外部工具专门设计的契约层。

**练习 2**：`dialect-mir` 的 IR 在哪一步会落盘成一个文件？

**参考答案**：默认不落盘。`dialect-mir` 只存在于内存里的 Pliron `Context` 中，被 `run_pipeline` 直接喂给后续 pass。落盘的产物是更下游的 `*.ll`（LLVM IR）与 `*.ptx`。要看 dialect-mir 文本需要打开 dump（见 4.4）。

---

### 4.2 mir-importer pipeline 阶段

#### 4.2.1 概念说明

`run_pipeline` 是 `mir-importer` 对外的「一键编译」入口。它把「一批已收集的设备函数（`CollectedFunction` 列表）」一路推到「PTX 或 NVVM IR 文件」。理解它的**阶段序列**，就理解了 `mir-importer` 在整条流水线里做了哪些事——以及哪些事其实不是它做的（lowering、llc 调用分别委托给了 `mir-lower` 与外部 `llc`）。

注意一个易混点：尽管函数叫 `run_pipeline`、文件叫 `pipeline.rs`，但**「translator 把 MIR 翻成 dialect-mir」只是其中第 2 步**。pipeline 还要负责注册方言、校验、mem2reg、循环展开、调用 lowering、导出文本、跑 llc。把这些全放在一个函数里，是为了让 `device_codegen` 只需一次调用就能拿到 PTX。

#### 4.2.2 核心流程

`run_pipeline` 的官方步骤序列（来自其文档注释）：

```text
1. 注册 dialect-mir / dialect-nvvm / LLVM 方言
2. 对每个函数：把 MIR body 翻译成 dialect-mir
3. 校验 dialect-mir 模块
4. （非 full-debug 时）跑 mem2reg，把 alloca+load/store 提升回 SSA
4.6 在 SSA 形态上跑「带注解的循环展开」（#[unroll]）
4.9 插入 device-extern 声明（供调用 lowering 时保留地址空间）
5. 调 mir-lower：dialect-mir → LLVM 方言
6. 校验 LLVM 方言模块
7. 导出为文本 LLVM IR（.ll）
8. 跑 llc 生成 PTX；或检测到 libdevice 时改走 NVVM IR（跳过 llc）
```

几条关键判断值得记住：

- **full-debug 跳过 mem2reg/unroll**：当 `debug_kind.variables_enabled()`（cuda-gdb 可读变量的 `-G` 风格构建）时，源变量必须留在稳定的栈槽里，所以跳过提升与展开。这一点 u7-l2 会用到。
- **libdevice 自动改道**：如果 lowering 后出现了 `__nv_*`（如 `__nv_sinf`），`llc` 无法解析，必须走 libNVVM + nvJitLink，于是 pipeline 改产出 NVVM IR（`.ll`）并跳过 llc。这会在 u4-l5 详讲。
- **目标架构自动探测**：pipeline 会在导出的 LLVM 文本里扫描 PTX 指令特征（WGMMA、TMA、tcgen05、bf16x2……），据此选 `sm_XX`，可用 `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET` 覆盖。

#### 4.2.3 源码精读

pipeline.rs 顶部的文档给出了与上面一致的步骤序列与一张「GPU 特征 → 目标架构」表：

[crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs:6-46](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L6-L46) —— 这张表是后续 u5（高级设备能力）各讲的「架构门禁」依据，例如 WGMMA→`sm_90a`、tcgen05→`sm_100a`、bf16 mma→`sm_80`。

`run_pipeline` 的签名与文档：

[crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs:248-281](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L248-L281) —— 三个入参：`functions`（设备函数集）、`device_externs`（外部设备函数声明，用于 LTOIR FFI）、`config`（`PipelineConfig`）。

第 2 步「逐函数翻译」的循环：

[crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs:300-351](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L300-L351) —— 对每个 `CollectedFunction` 取出 `instance.body()`，调 `translator::body::translate_body`，dump（若开启）、`verify`、再 `append_to_module`。注意 dump 故意放在 verify **之前**，这样即使校验失败，用户也能在 `--show-mir-dialect` 里看到出问题的 IR。

第 4 / 4.6 步的 mem2reg + 循环展开：

[crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs:367-425](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L367-L425) —— 关键分支是 `if config.debug_kind.variables_enabled()` 决定**跳过** mem2reg；否则跑 pliron 的 `mem2reg`，再跑 `mir_transforms::unroll::unroll_annotated_loops`。注释点明了「full-debug 把变量留在内存里以便 cuda-gdb 读取」的动机。

第 5 步调用 lowering：

[crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs:443-447](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L443-L447) —— 一行 `lower_to_llvm(...)` 把 `dialect-mir` 交给 `mir-lower`，并把 `allow_fma_contraction` 透传下去（这条线在 u6-l1/u6-l3 与 FMA 收缩策略相关）。

第 7、8 步的导出与 PTX/NVVM 分叉：

[crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs:537-624](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L537-L624) —— `emit_nvvm_ir` 为真时跳过 llc、产出 `.ll` 作为 NVVM IR 制品；否则调 `generate_ptx` 跑 llc 得到 `.ptx`。`CompilationResult` 里同时带上了 `allow_fma_contraction`，供制品边车记录（接 u3-l2）。

5. 「待本地验证」：本实践无需运行命令。

#### 4.2.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：`run_pipeline` 里，mem2reg 跑在 lowering 之前还是之后？为什么必须在那个位置？

**参考答案**：之前。mem2reg 把 translator 产生的 `alloca + load/store` 槽位提升回 SSA，正是为了给 lowering 一个「干净的 SSA 输入」；若放在 lowering 之后，LLVM 方言里已经没有 `mir.alloca` 这种 op 可供提升了。

**练习 2**：为什么 full-debug 构建要跳过 mem2reg？

**参考答案**：mem2reg 会把每个源变量从「稳定的栈槽」收窄到「寄存器的活跃区间」，cuda-gdb 在优化构建里就会显示 `<optimized out>`。full-debug 希望变量在整个作用域内都可从固定内存地址读出，所以必须保留栈槽，自然就跳过提升（也跳过循环展开）。

---

### 4.3 translator 分层与 alloca + load/store 模型

#### 4.3.1 概念说明

`run_pipeline` 的第 2 步背后，是 `translator` 模块。它采用**与 MIR 结构一一对应的分层**：

| translator 子模块 | 对应 MIR 概念 | 职责 |
|---|---|---|
| `body` | 整个函数体 | 建函数、建块、entry allocas、遍历可达块 |
| `block` | 一个基本块 | 按序翻译语句，最后翻译终止符 |
| `statement` | 语句（`Assign` 等） | 赋值、投影、存储标记 |
| `rvalue` | 右值（表达式） | 二元运算、cast、聚合等 → dialect-mir op |
| `terminator` | 终止符 | 控制流 + GPU intrinsic 分派 |
| `values` | 局部变量 | local → alloca 槽位映射 |
| `types` | 类型 | Rust 类型 → dialect-mir 类型 |

贯穿全层的是 **alloca + load/store 模型**。它的核心思想非常简单：

> 每个非 ZST 的 MIR local，都在函数入口块顶部申请一个栈槽 `mir.alloca`。对该 local 的「写」变成 `mir.store` 到这个槽，「读」变成 `mir.load` 从这个槽。**跨基本块的数据流一律走槽位，块之间不传参。**

这样做的好处是：translator 不需要在翻译每个块时都维护一套 SSA 构造算法（SSA 构造是出了名的麻烦），只需要「写槽 / 读槽」两条简单规则；把「槽位 → SSA」这件难事交给成熟的 `mem2reg` pass 在第 4 步统一完成。

#### 4.3.2 核心流程

一个 MIR 函数被翻译的完整调用链（来自 `translator/mod.rs` 的文档）：

```text
translate_function()                       // 入口：建 builtin.module
  └─▶ body::translate_body()               // 函数级
        ├─▶ emit_entry_allocas()           // 每个 non-ZST local 一个 alloca
        └─▶ 对每个可达块：
              └─▶ block::translate_block()
                    ├─▶ statement::translate_statement()   // 逐条语句
                    │     └─▶ rvalue::translate_rvalue()   // 右值
                    └─▶ terminator::translate_terminator() // 末尾终止符
```

数据如何在块间流动？用 terminator/mod.rs 文档里那个经典例子最直观：

```text
// Rust MIR
bb0: { _1 = 42_i32; goto -> bb1 }
bb1: { _0 = _1;     return }

// dialect-mir（mem2reg 之前）
^bb0:
  %s1 = mir.alloca          : !mir.ptr<i32>
  %c  = mir.constant 42_i32 : i32
  mir.store %c, %s1
  mir.goto ^bb1                    // 零操作数；_1 经 %s1 流动
^bb1:                              // 没有块参数
  %r = mir.load %s1 : i32
  mir.return %r : i32

// mem2reg 之后塌缩成
//   mir.return %c : i32
```

这个例子同时回答了两个问题：块间怎么传数据（走槽位）、mem2reg 做了什么（把槽位往返折叠成 SSA）。

#### 4.3.3 源码精读

`translator/mod.rs` 顶部的模块结构表与调用流程图：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs:11-44](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs#L11-L44) —— 这张表对应上面的分层表，并把 alloca 模型再强调了一遍。

`register_dialects`：translator 开始前必须把会用到的方言都注册进 `Context`：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs:67-80](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs#L67-L80) —— 注册 `dialect-mir`（建模 Rust 语义）与 `dialect-nvvm`（GPU intrinsic，如 thread/block/warp）。注释说明 builtin 方言（`ModuleOp` 等）由 pliron 0.14 自动注册。

`translate_function`：对外「单函数翻译」入口（主要供测试/工具用，真实 pipeline 走的是 `body::translate_body`）：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs:90-150](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/mod.rs#L90-L150) —— 它建一个 `builtin.module`，把 `translate_body` 产出的 `mir.func` 塞进去。注意它固定 `is_inline_always = false`（真实 pipeline 会从 rustc 的 `CodegenFnAttrs` 取这个标志并透传，接 u6-l1）。

alloca 模型的「槽位账本」`ValueMap`：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/values.rs:52-87](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/values.rs#L52-L87) —— 一个 `Vec<Option<Value>>`，按下标对应 MIR local 编号；ZST local 留 `None`。`get_slot`/`set_slot` 是它的读写接口。

三个 slot 操作的发 emitter：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/values.rs:96-172](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/values.rs#L96-L172) —— `emit_alloca` 发 `mir.alloca`，`load_local` 发 `mir.load`，`store_local` 发 `mir.store`。`store_local` 还会在指针地址空间不一致时自动插一个 `mir.cast <PtrToPtr>`（见 `maybe_ptr_coerce`），这是 GPU 地址空间（shared/global/constant）正确性的关键，与 u2-l3 共享内存、u5 系列直接相关。

`emit_entry_allocas` 的文档把 alloca 模型讲得最完整：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs:514-541](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L514-L541) —— 函数入口块顶部，对每个 non-ZST local 发一个 alloca、记录到 `value_map`，再把函数参数 `store` 进各自的槽；返回最后一条 op，供后续语句「接在后面」追加（否则会被 `insert_at_front` 推到 alloca 链前面）。

各分层模块的「能力表」也很值得扫一眼，能快速知道哪些 Rust 写法已被支持：

- 语句表：[statement.rs:10-35](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/statement.rs#L10-L35)（`Assign`/`StorageLive`/`SetDiscriminant`/`Nop`，以及 1～2 级投影的专用路径）。
- 右值表：[rvalue.rs:10-31](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/rvalue.rs#L10-L31)（`BinaryOp`→`mir.add/sub/mul/div`、`Cast`→`mir.cast`、`Aggregate`→`mir.construct_*` 等）。

#### 4.3.4 代码实践

源码阅读 + 对照式实践：用一个真实内核，把它的 Rust 写法对应到 translator 各层的 op。

1. **实践目标**：对一个最简内核（vecadd），手工把它的每条语句「投射」到 translator 的分层表上。
2. **操作步骤**：
   - 打开 [examples/vecadd/src/main.rs:39-46](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/examples/vecadd/src/main.rs#L39-L46) 的 `vecadd` 内核。
   - 逐句判断它会落到哪个分层模块：`thread::index_1d()` → `terminator`（intrinsic 分派，落到 `dialect-nvvm` 的特殊寄存器 op）；`a[idx] + b[idx]` → `rvalue`（`mir.add`）；`*c_elem = ...` → `statement`（投影写）。
3. **需要观察的现象**：你会发现「读线程号」和「算加法」分属两个不同的分层模块，前者还要跨到 `dialect-nvvm`。
4. **预期结果**：你能画出 vecadd 内核对应的「translator 调用链」草图。
5. 「待本地验证」：本实践无需运行命令；真实的 op 文本在 4.4 的 pipeline 实践里观察。

#### 4.3.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：为什么 translator 不直接构造 SSA，而要绕一圈「alloca + load/store 再 mem2reg」？

**参考答案**：因为 SSA 构造（需要支配边界、φ 节点插入点等）实现复杂、易错。alloca + load/store 是一个**机械、局部**的翻译方案：每个 local 一个槽、写即 store、读即 load，不需要在翻译时做任何跨块分析。把「提升回 SSA」这件难事交给成熟、可复用的 `mem2reg` pass，换来 translator 的简单与正确。

**练习 2**：`store_local` 为什么会在某些情况下自动插入一个 `mir.cast <PtrToPtr>`？

**参考答案**：Rust 的引用/裸指针类型本身不携带 GPU 地址空间信息（`&mut f32` 翻译出来是 generic 指针），但实际写入槽位的值可能是具体地址空间的指针（如 `addrspace(3)` 的共享内存指针）。两者指针布局相同但类型不同，于是用一个免费的 `PtrToPtr` cast 桥接，避免后续 lowering 走到错误的（generic）访存路径。

### 4.4 MIR 基本块映射到 Pliron IR

#### 4.4.1 概念说明

translator 要解决的最后一个结构性问题是：**rustc MIR 的基本块图，怎么变成 Pliron IR 的基本块图？** 这里有三个细节决定了正确性：

1. **可达性**：MIR 里有些块只是 unwind cleanup（栈展开清理），而 CUDA 工具链根本不支持栈展开——这些块在 GPU 上是死代码。translator 用 BFS 从入口块出发，只翻译真正可达的块。
2. **块参数**：因为 alloca 模型让数据走槽位，**只有入口块带参数**（函数形参），其余块都是零参数的。这让所有分支终止符都是「零操作数」的，结构极简。
3. **死块的兜底**：Pliron 要求每个块都有终止符。被跳过的 unwind 块不能空着，translator 给它们补一个 `mir.unreachable`，让校验通过，后续 pass 可把它们当死代码删掉。

#### 4.4.2 核心流程

`translate_body` 把 MIR 块图搬进 Pliron 的三阶段（来自其文档）：

```text
PHASE 1   建 Pliron 块：每个 MIR 块一个 Pliron 块；仅入口块带函数形参
PHASE 1.5 entry allocas：入口块顶部为每个 non-ZST local 发 alloca，存入函数参数
PHASE 2   按「可达块」集合逐块翻译（block::translate_block）
          不可达的 unwind 块补 mir.unreachable
```

可达块的计算是一个从入口块（索引 0）出发、沿「非 unwind 后继」做 BFS 的过程；unwind cleanup 块最终落在可达集之外。

#### 4.4.3 源码精读

BFS 可达性分析：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs:191-210](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L191-L210) —— `compute_reachable_blocks` 从入口块出发，调 `non_unwind_successors` 收集后继。`non_unwind_successors`（[body.rs:172-189](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L172-L189)）的关键判断是：`Drop`/`Call`/`Assert` 等终止符的 unwind 目标被剔除，只保留「正常」目标。注释点明 CUDA 工具链不接栈展开。

PHASE 1 + PHASE 1.5 + PHASE 2 的主循环：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs:873-965](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L873-L965) —— 注意三处：建块时只有 `idx == 0` 用 `arg_types`（[L882-887](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L882-L887)）；entry allocas 在 [L909](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L909) 调 `emit_entry_allocas`；可达块逐块翻译在 [L931-947](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L931-L947)；最后给未处理块补 `mir.unreachable` 在 [L953-965](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L953-L965)。

单块内容翻译（先语句后终止符）：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/block.rs:30-77](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/block.rs#L30-L77) —— `translate_block` 用 `prev_op` 这条「游标」把语句一条条 `insert_after` 串起来，保证 IR 文本顺序与 MIR 一致；终止符最后翻译。注释强调「块本身由 `body` 创建，本模块只负责填充内容」。

`translate_body` 的总体文档（含「单元返回变成 void 签名」等细节）：

[crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs:608-640](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L608-L640) —— 还能看到 `#[kernel]` 时给 `mir.func` 加 `gpu_kernel` 属性、扫描 `#[cluster(...)]`/`#[launch_bounds(...)]` 标记注入 `cluster_dim_*`/`maxntid` 属性（接 u2-l4、u5-l4、u7-l2）。

#### 4.4.4 代码实践（本讲主实践）

这是本讲唯一需要实际运行（或阅读运行逻辑）的实践：用 `cargo oxide pipeline` 把 vecadd 的 dialect-mir 中间产物「显形」，对照 `pipeline.rs` 说明它经过了哪几个阶段。

1. **实践目标**：亲眼看到 translator 产出的 `dialect-mir`（mem2reg 前后两份）、lowering 后的 LLVM IR、最终 PTX，并把它们对回 `run_pipeline` 的步骤编号。
2. **操作步骤**：
   - 进入仓库根目录，运行：
     ```bash
     cargo oxide pipeline vecadd
     ```
   - `pipeline` 子命令（`cargo-oxide` 的 `codegen_show_pipeline`）会自动打开若干诊断开关，其中关键是：
     - `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_MIR=1` → 经后端映射成 `PipelineConfig::show_mir_dialect=true`，让 `run_pipeline` 把 dialect-mir 文本打印到 **stderr**（见 [commands.rs:1675](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/cargo-oxide/src/commands.rs#L1675) 与 [device_codegen.rs:563-687](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/rustc-codegen-cuda/src/device_codegen.rs#L563-L687)）。
     - `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_LLVM=1` → 打印 LLVM 方言模块。
   - 构建结束后，`pipeline` 会把落盘产物打印到 stdout：`vecadd.ll`（LLVM IR）与 `vecadd.ptx`（PTX），位于示例目录下（见 [commands.rs:3022-3047](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/cargo-oxide/src/commands.rs#L3022-L3047) 的 `show_generated_artifacts`）。
3. **需要观察的现象**：在 stderr 里应能看到形如下面的阶段标题（由 [pipeline.rs:355-365](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L355-L365) 与 [L401-404](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/pipeline.rs#L401-L404) 打印）：
   - `=== dialect-mir module (pre-verify) ===`（第 2 步 translator 产物）
   - `=== Running mem2reg ===` + `=== dialect-mir module (after mem2reg) ===`（第 4 步）
   - `=== Lowering dialect-mir → LLVM dialect ===`（第 5 步）
   - `=== Generating PTX ===`（第 8 步）
4. **预期结果**：你能把 stderr 里每个阶段标题对应到 `run_pipeline` 文档里的一个步骤号，并在 `vecadd.ll` 里看到 `mir.func vecadd` 已经被 lower 成 `define void @vecadd(...)`，在 `vecadd.ptx` 里看到 `.entry vecadd`。
5. 「待本地验证」：上面所有「应能看到」的具体输出文本均依赖本机具备 nightly 工具链与 `llc-21+`（见 u1-l3）。若本机缺 GPU，`pipeline` 仍可工作（它只编译不运行内核）；若工具链不全，则按 u1-l3 的 `cargo oxide doctor` 先补齐。若无法运行，请改为阅读 `run_pipeline` 源码逐段确认步骤。

#### 4.4.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：假如一个内核用了 `Result` 且写了 `?`，MIR 里会带 unwind cleanup 块。translator 怎么处理它们？

**参考答案**：`non_unwind_successors` 在收集后继时把 `Call`/`Drop` 等终止符的 unwind 目标剔除，`compute_reachable_blocks` 的 BFS 因此不会遍历到这些 cleanup 块。它们落在可达集之外，被 [body.rs:953-965](https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/blob/2b713541cb572517b4932a50b4f4087ffb66203d/crates/mir-importer/src/translator/body.rs#L953-L965) 补上一个 `mir.unreachable` 终止符以满足 Pliron 校验，后续 pass 会把它们当死代码删掉。

**练习 2**：为什么除了入口块，所有 Pliron 块都是「零参数」的？

**参考答案**：因为 alloca 模型让跨块数据流全部走 local 的栈槽（`mir.store`/`mir.load`），不需要在块之间显式传值。只有入口块需要带参数——那是函数形参的落脚点，`emit_entry_allocas` 会立刻把它们 store 进各自槽位。这同时让所有分支终止符（`mir.goto`/`mir.cond_br`/`mir.switch`）都是零操作数的，结构最简。

---

## 5. 综合实践

把本讲四个模块串起来，做一个「静态追踪 + 动态对照」的小任务：给 vecadd 的内核**加一个条件分支**，然后观察它在四个阶段里的形态变化。

具体步骤：

1. 复制 vecadd 示例（或直接改 `examples/vecadd/src/main.rs` 的 `vecadd` 内核），把内核体改成带一个 `if idx_raw % 2 == 0` 的分支，例如偶数线程做加法、奇数线程做减法：
   ```rust
   #[kernel]
   pub fn vecadd(a: &[f32], b: &[f32], mut c: DisjointSlice<f32>) {
       let idx = thread::index_1d();
       let idx_raw = idx.get();
       if let Some(c_elem) = c.get_mut(idx) {
           if idx_raw % 2 == 0 {
               *c_elem = a[idx_raw] + b[idx_raw];
           } else {
               *c_elem = a[idx_raw] - b[idx_raw];
           }
       }
   }
   ```
   > 这是「示例代码」，仅用于说明；真实 vecadd 示例不含此分支。
2. 运行 `cargo oxide pipeline vecadd`，在 stderr 的 `dialect-mir module` dump 里找到你的 `mir.func`。
3. 验证你对本讲的掌握，回答：
   - **4.1（Pliron 载体）**：这个带分支的内核，`if` 在 MIR 里通常变成 `SwitchInt` 终止符。它会被哪个分层模块翻译？预期在 dialect-mir 里看到哪种 op？（提示：`terminator/mod.rs`，`mir.cond_br`。）
   - **4.2（pipeline）**：分支会让块变多，但不会改变阶段序列。确认你仍能看到 `pre-verify → mem2reg → after mem2reg → lowering → PTX` 这条标题链。
   - **4.3（alloca 模型）**：在 `pre-verify` 的 dump 里，应能看到 `mir.alloca`、`mir.store`、`mir.load`；在 `after mem2reg` 里它们应被折叠成 SSA 值（不再有针对标量 local 的 load/store 往返）。对照 terminator/mod.rs 的经典例子说明你看到了同样的「塌缩」。
   - **4.4（块映射）**：分支应在 dialect-mir 里产生至少两个目标块；确认 `mir.cond_br` 是零操作数的（数据靠槽位流动，不靠块参数）。
4. 最后核对正确性：若本机有 GPU，`cargo oxide run vecadd` 应输出「偶数下标相加、奇数下标相减」的结果。

> 这个任务同时触动了本讲的全部四个最小模块，并自然地为下一讲（u4-l3 方言层）与 u6-l2（terminator 深潜）埋下伏笔——你会开始好奇 `SwitchInt` 到底分派到哪个 dialect op。

## 6. 本讲小结

- `mir-importer` 的职责是**把 rustc 的 stable MIR 翻译成 Pliron 的 `dialect-mir`**，并编排其后的 mem2reg、循环展开、lowering、PTX 导出；但 lowering 本身委托给 `mir-lower`，PTX 生成委托给外部 `llc`。
- `run_pipeline` 是一条清晰的 8 步流水线：注册方言 → 逐函数翻译 → 校验 → mem2reg → 循环展开 → lowering → 校验 → 导出 `.ll` → 跑 `llc` 出 PTX（或检测到 libdevice 改走 NVVM IR）。
- translator 采用**与 MIR 一一对应的分层**（body / block / statement / rvalue / terminator / values / types），并贯穿 **alloca + load/store 模型**：每个 non-ZST local 一个栈槽，写即 store、读即 load，把 SSA 构造这件难事推迟给 mem2reg。
- MIR 块图的映射有三个要点：BFS 只翻译可达块（unwind cleanup 被当死代码）、仅入口块带函数形参、不可达块补 `mir.unreachable` 兜底校验。
- `dialect-mir` 默认只在内存里；要看它需用 `cargo oxide pipeline`（打开 `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_MIR`），落盘的可见产物是下游的 `.ll` 与 `.ptx`。
- 本讲全程是「鸟瞰」：单条 MIR 语句如何落到具体 op、terminator 如何分派 GPU intrinsic、lowering 内部细节，分别留给 u6-l2 与 u6-l3。

## 7. 下一步学习建议

- **u4-l3（MLIR 方言层）**：本讲反复提到 `dialect-mir` 与 `dialect-nvvm`，下一讲正式拆解这两个方言各自提供哪些 op、它们如何承接「Rust 语义 → GPU 指令」的过渡。
- **u4-l4（MIR Lowering 鸟瞰）**：本讲的第 5 步 `lower_to_llvm` 把控制权交给了 `mir-lower`，下一讲鸟览那个 crate 的 ops/intrinsics 两类转换入口。
- **u6-l2（mir-importer 深潜：terminator/intrinsics 翻译机）**：当你想知道 `thread::index_1d()`、`atom_add`、`mma` 等具体如何在 `terminator/mod.rs` 里被分派成方言 op 时，去读这一篇。
- **建议继续阅读的源码**：想加深对 alloca 模型的体感，推荐读 `translator/values.rs` 的 `ValueMap` 与 `maybe_ptr_coerce`；想理解可达性剪枝，读 `translator/body.rs` 的 `non_unwind_successors` + `compute_reachable_blocks`。
